  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the purchase order. Used for ordering and referencing specific line items within the PO.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this purchase order line. Tracks progression from open through receipt to closure or cancellation.']
  liquidated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative amount of encumbrance that has been liquidated (released) through receipts and invoices. Used for encumbrance tracking and budget reconciliation.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'The name of the manufacturer of the goods being procured. Used for quality tracking and warranty management.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturer\'s part or model number for the item. Used for precise item identification and warranty claims.']
  need_by_date date [note: 'The date by which the goods or services are required by the requesting department. Used for delivery scheduling and vendor performance tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional instructions, specifications, or comments related to this purchase order line.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Program or functional area code for tracking expenditures by institutional program or initiative. Part of the chartfield distribution.']
  promised_delivery_date date [note: 'The date the vendor has committed to deliver the goods or complete the services. Used for vendor performance evaluation.']
  quantity_invoiced decimal(15,3) [note: 'The cumulative quantity that has been invoiced by the vendor against this line to date. Used for three-way match validation.']
  quantity_ordered decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of goods or services ordered on this line, expressed in the unit of measure. Original order quantity before any receipts or adjustments.']
  quantity_received decimal(15,3) [note: 'The cumulative quantity of goods or services that have been received against this line to date. Used for three-way match validation.']
  receipt_required_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a formal receipt transaction is required before payment can be processed. True for goods, typically false for services.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total tax amount applicable to this line (sales tax, use tax, VAT). Calculated based on tax jurisdiction and item taxability.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the quantity is measured (e.g., each, box, hour, pound). Defines how the item is counted or measured for procurement purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|box|case|dozen|hour|day|month|year|pound|kilogram|liter|gallon — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The price per unit of measure for the item being procured. Negotiated or catalog price before any discounts or taxes.']
  vendor_item_number varchar [note: 'The vendor\'s catalog or part number for this item. Used for order accuracy and vendor communication.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a purchase order specifying the goods or services being procured. Captures line number, item description, commodity code, unit of measure, quantity ordered, unit price, extended amount, chartfield distribution (fund, cost center, account, project), receipt status, and invoiced amount. Supports three-way match (PO, receipt, invoice) and encumbrance liquidation in PeopleSoft Financials.'
}

Table finance.ap_invoice {
  ap_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the accounts payable invoice record. Primary key.']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.capital_project. Business justification: Capital construction/renovation invoices (AIA payment applications, contractor billings) must reference the capital project for project budget tracking, draw schedule management, and GASB 34 capitaliz']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: AP invoices are charged to organizational cost centers. Adding cost_center_id FK allows joining to cost_center master for cost_center_name, hierarchy, manager, and budget attributes. Removes redundant']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advancement.event. Business justification: Advancement event expense tracking: fundraising and stewardship events (galas, donor recognition dinners) generate AP invoices for vendor services. Finance codes these invoices to the specific advance']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: AP invoices are charged to funds (self-balancing accounting entities). Adding fund_id FK allows joining to finance_fund master for fund_name, fund_type, net_asset_classification, and restriction attri']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: AP invoices are posted to fiscal periods for accounting cycle management. Adding fiscal_period_id FK allows joining to fiscal_period master for period_name, period dates, status flags, and reporting a']
  grant_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the research grant or sponsored project if this invoice is charged to grant funds. Null for non-grant invoices.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: AP invoices generate journal entries when posted to the general ledger. Adding journal_entry_id FK (nullable) allows linking to the GL posting. Populated during posting process. No columns removed as ']
  ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger_account. Business justification: AP invoices are charged to GL accounts. Adding ledger_account_id FK allows joining to ledger_account master for account_name, account_type, hierarchy, and classification attributes. Removes redundant ']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order against which this invoice is matched. Null for non-PO invoices.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: AP invoices for federally-funded expenditures must demonstrate allowability under specific regulatory requirements (2 CFR 200 Uniform Guidance). Single Audit procedures require tracing invoices to gov']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor who submitted this invoice for payment.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.work_order. Business justification: Contractor and vendor invoices for facilities maintenance and repair must be matched to the originating work order for three-way match (PO/receipt/invoice), cost-per-work-order reporting, and faciliti']
  ap_invoice_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the goods or services covered by this invoice.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the invoice in the approval workflow.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the invoice for payment.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was approved for payment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this invoice record was first created in the AP system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the invoice amount.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Early payment discount amount available if paid by discount due date.']
  discount_due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment must be made to qualify for early payment discount. Null if no discount offered.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the invoice is disputed. Null if not disputed.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment is due to the vendor per the payment terms.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied for foreign currency invoices to convert to institutional base currency.']
  form_1099_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the invoice amount that is reportable on IRS Form 1099.']
  form_1099_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice amount is reportable on IRS Form 1099 for the vendor.']
  freight_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Shipping and freight charges included in the invoice.']
  gross_invoice_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total invoice amount before any discounts or adjustments.']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the invoice is on hold and not being processed for payment. Null if not on hold.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The date the vendor issued the invoice. Used for aging analysis and payment term calculation.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'The vendor-assigned invoice number as printed on the invoice document. Business identifier for external reference.']
  invoice_received_date date [note: 'The date the institution received the invoice from the vendor.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice in the accounts payable workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: entered|matched|approved|paid|on_hold|cancelled|disputed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice transaction type.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who last modified this invoice record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this invoice record was last updated.']
  match_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice has been matched to a purchase order and/or receipt (two-way or three-way match).']
  net_invoice_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final payable amount after applying discounts, taxes, and adjustments.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date payment was issued to the vendor. Null if not yet paid.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The method used to pay the invoice.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Check number, ACH trace number, or wire confirmation number for the payment.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The payment terms specified by the vendor (e.g., Net 30, 2/10 Net 30). Defines discount eligibility and due date calculation.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'The academic or administrative program code to which this expense is allocated.']
  remit_to_location varchar [note: 'The vendor location or address to which payment should be remitted.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount charged on the invoice (sales tax, use tax, VAT).']
  voucher_number varchar [note: 'Internal voucher or document number assigned by the AP system for tracking and audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Accounts payable invoice record representing a vendor\'s request for payment for goods or services delivered to the institution. Captures invoice number, invoice date, vendor reference, PO reference, invoice amount, tax amount, discount amount, due date, payment terms, invoice status (entered, matched, approved, paid, on hold), approval workflow status, and 1099 reportable flag. Sourced from Oracle PeopleSoft Financials AP module. Supports three-way match and GASB expense recognition.'
}

Table finance.ap_payment {
  ap_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the accounts payable payment disbursement record. Primary key.']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the institutional bank account from which the payment was disbursed.']

Adding cost_center_id FK allows joining to cost_center master for cost_center_name, hierarchy, manager, and budget attributes. Removes redundant']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: AP payments are charged to funds (self-balancing accounting entities). Adding fund_id FK allows joining to finance_fund master for fund_name, fund_type, net_asset_classification, and restriction attri']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: AP payments are posted to fiscal periods for accounting cycle management. Adding fiscal_period_id FK allows joining to fiscal_period master for period_name, period dates, status flags, and reporting a']
  grant_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the research grant or sponsored project if the payment is charged to grant funds. Links to grant accounting records.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: AP payments generate journal entries when posted to the general ledger. Adding journal_entry_id FK (nullable) allows linking to the GL posting. Populated during posting process. No columns removed as ']
  primary_ap_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee or user who approved the payment for disbursement.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vendor or supplier receiving the payment. Links to the vendor master record.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'The name of the employee or user who approved the payment for disbursement.']
  check_number varchar [note: 'The physical check number if the payment method is check. Null for electronic payment methods.']
  cleared_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment cleared the bank and was reconciled. Used for cash flow tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the payment record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  discount_taken_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of early payment discount taken by the institution, reducing the net payment amount.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the user who last modified the payment record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the payment record was last updated or modified. Audit trail for record changes.']
  net_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final net amount disbursed after applying discounts and adjustments. Represents the actual cash outflow.']
  payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total gross amount of the payment disbursed to the vendor before any adjustments.']
  payment_approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment was approved for disbursement by an authorized approver.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment was issued or disbursed to the vendor. Principal business event timestamp for the payment transaction.']
  payment_description varchar [note: 'Textual description or memo explaining the purpose or nature of the payment.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The instrument or mechanism used to disburse the payment (check, ACH/EFT, wire transfer, purchasing card).']
  payment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique payment reference number assigned by the Oracle PeopleSoft Financials AP Payment module. Used for tracking and reconciliation.']
  payment_source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the source system or module that originated the payment record (e.g., Oracle PeopleSoft Financials AP, Workday Financials).']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment disbursement indicating its processing state.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment by its business purpose or nature (regular vendor payment, refund, advance, employee reimbursement).']
  preparer_name varchar [note: 'The name of the employee or user who prepared or initiated the payment record.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'The academic or administrative program code associated with the payment, supporting program-level financial analysis.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment was reconciled with the bank statement.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the payment has been reconciled with the bank statement.']
  remittance_advice_sent_date date [note: 'The date on which remittance advice was sent to the vendor.']
  remittance_advice_sent_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether remittance advice was sent to the vendor notifying them of the payment details.']
  tax_reporting_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this payment requires tax reporting (e.g., 1099 reporting for vendors).']
  vendor_site_code varchar [note: 'The specific vendor site or location code to which the payment is directed, supporting multi-location vendor management.']
  void_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment was voided or cancelled. Null if payment was not voided.']
  void_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or explanation for voiding the payment (e.g., duplicate payment, vendor request, incorrect amount).']

  Note: 'Accounts payable payment disbursement record capturing actual payments made to vendors. Records payment reference number, payment date, payment method (check, ACH/EFT, wire transfer), bank account, payment amount, discount taken, vendor reference, invoice references paid, payment status (issued, cleared, voided, stale-dated), and bank reconciliation status. Sourced from Oracle PeopleSoft Financials AP Payment module.'
}

Table finance.ar_invoice {

Adding ar_ledger_account_id FK allows joining to ledger_account master for account_name, account_type, hierarchy, and classification attributes. Removes red']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: AR invoices are credited to organizational cost centers. Adding cost_center_id FK allows joining to cost_center master for cost_center_name, hierarchy, manager, and budget attributes. Removes redundan']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: AR invoices are credited to funds (self-balancing accounting entities). Adding fund_id FK allows joining to finance_fund master for fund_name, fund_type, net_asset_classification, and restriction attr']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: AR invoices must be associated with a fiscal period for revenue recognition, period-end close, and GASB/FASB reporting. The ar_invoice table has invoice_date and revenue_recognition_date but no fiscal']
  grant_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sponsored research grant or contract if this invoice represents grant-related billing (e.g., F&A cost recovery, direct cost reimbursement). Links to the research grant entity.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: AR invoices generate journal entries when posted to the general ledger. Adding journal_entry_id FK (nullable) allows linking to the GL posting. Populated during posting process. No columns removed as ']
  research_award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.research_award. Business justification: AR invoices for sponsored research are generated against specific research awards for cost reimbursement billing. This link supports award-level billing status tracking, invoice-to-award reconciliatio']
  room_booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.room_booking. Business justification: External event/conference room rentals generate AR invoices billed to outside organizations. Higher-ed conference services and auxiliary operations require the AR invoice to reference the room booking']
  sponsor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.sponsor. Business justification: Universities bill sponsors via AR invoices for sponsored project cost reimbursement and drawdowns. The customer_name plain attribute is a denormalization signal. Linking AR invoices to sponsors suppor']
  aging_bucket varchar [note: 'Classification of invoice age relative to due date for accounts receivable aging analysis. Used to assess collectability and allowance for doubtful accounts.']
  amount_outstanding decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining unpaid balance on the invoice. Calculated as net_amount minus amount_paid.']
  amount_paid decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount paid against this invoice to date. Used to calculate outstanding balance and track partial payments.']
  ar_invoice_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the goods or services being invoiced. Provides context for the charges and supports customer inquiries.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the customer billing address (street number and name). Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the customer billing address (suite, floor, building). Optional field for additional address details.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City name for the customer billing address.']
  billing_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the billing contact for invoice delivery and payment communication. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  billing_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person at the customer organization for billing inquiries and payment coordination.']
  billing_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the billing contact for invoice inquiries and payment coordination. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the customer billing address.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the customer billing address.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code for the customer billing address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this invoice record was first created in the AR system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the invoice amounts. Most higher education institutions operate primarily in USD but may invoice international customers in other currencies.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount amount applied to the invoice. May include early payment discounts, volume discounts, or negotiated rate reductions.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is expected based on invoice date and payment terms. Used for aging calculations and collections prioritization.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total invoice amount before tax and adjustments. Represents the base charges for goods or services provided.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was issued to the customer. Represents the business event timestamp for revenue recognition and aging calculations.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique invoice number assigned by the AR system. Used for customer communication and payment reference.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice indicating payment state and collectability.']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice transaction type. Standard invoices represent new charges; credit memos reduce amounts owed; debit memos increase amounts owed.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this invoice record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this invoice record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment received against this invoice. Used for payment tracking and customer payment behavior analysis.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount due from the customer after applying tax and discounts. Calculated as gross_amount + tax_amount - discount_amount.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Preferred or expected payment instrument for this invoice. Indicates how the customer typically remits payment.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual payment terms specifying when payment is due. Common terms include net 30 (due in 30 days) or 2/10 net 30 (2% discount if paid within 10 days, otherwise due in 30 days). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: net_15|net_30|net_45|net_60|net_90|due_on_receipt|2_10_net_30 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Program or project chartfield identifying the specific initiative or activity generating this revenue. Used for program-level financial analysis.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number such as customer purchase order number, contract number, or requisition number. Used for customer reconciliation and payment matching.']
  revenue_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code for the revenue recognition. Maps to the chart of accounts for financial reporting and revenue classification.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date on which revenue from this invoice is recognized in the general ledger. May differ from invoice date based on revenue recognition policies and service delivery timing.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to the invoice. May include sales tax, use tax, or VAT depending on jurisdiction and customer tax status.']
  writeoff_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was written off as uncollectible. Populated only when invoice status is written_off.']
  writeoff_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for writing off the invoice. Used for bad debt analysis and collections process improvement.']

  Note: 'Accounts receivable invoice for non-student institutional receivables billed to external parties (government agencies, corporations, foundations, other universities, and auxiliary enterprise customers). Captures invoice number, date, customer/payor, billing address, amount, tax, due date, payment terms, AR account, fund and cost center chartfield, invoice status (open, partially paid, paid, written off), aging bucket, and revenue recognition date. Distinct from student billing invoices owned by the billing domain. Sourced from Oracle PeopleSoft Financials AR module.'
}

Table finance.ar_receipt {
  ar_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the accounts receivable cash receipt record. Primary key for the AR receipt entity.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member or supervisor who approved the receipt for posting, if approval workflow is required.']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the institutional bank account into which the payment was deposited.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: AR receipts are credited to organizational cost centers. Adding cost_center_id FK allows joining to cost_center master for cost_center_name, hierarchy, manager, and budget attributes. Removes redundan']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: AR receipts are credited to funds (self-balancing accounting entities). Adding fund_id FK allows joining to finance_fund master for fund_name, fund_type, net_asset_classification, and restriction attr']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: AR receipts are posted to fiscal periods for accounting cycle management. Adding fiscal_period_id FK allows joining to fiscal_period master for period_name, period dates, status flags, and reporting a']
  grant_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the grant or sponsored project to which the receipt is attributed, if applicable for grant-funded activities.']

Adding journal_entry_id FK (nullable) allows linking to the GL posting. Populated during posting process. No columns removed as ']
  primary_ar_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member or system user who entered or prepared the receipt record in the AR system.']
  research_award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.research_award. Business justification: Sponsor payments (AR receipts) are applied against research awards for cash receipt tracking and award balance management. Grants accountants require this link to reconcile sponsor payments against aw']
  sponsor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.sponsor. Business justification: AR receipts from sponsors must be traced to the sponsor for cash management, sponsor relationship reporting, and SAM/debarment compliance verification. The customer_name plain attribute is a denormali']
  applied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the receipt amount that has been applied to outstanding AR invoices or charges.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the receipt was approved for posting by the designated approver.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the staff member who approved the receipt, captured for audit and accountability purposes.']
  bank_deposit_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier from the bank deposit slip or electronic deposit confirmation, used for bank reconciliation.']
  check_number varchar [note: 'Check number provided by the customer when payment method is check, used for tracking and reconciliation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the receipt record was first created in the AR system, supporting audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which the payment was received (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  deposit_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment was deposited into the institutional bank account.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount amount allowed to the customer at the time of payment, such as early payment discounts or negotiated settlement reductions.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified the receipt record, supporting audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the receipt record was last updated or modified in the AR system, supporting change tracking and audit compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the receipt, such as payment exceptions or follow-up actions.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'The interface or channel through which the payment was submitted by the customer (e.g., online portal, mail, in-person, phone, mobile app).']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The instrument or mechanism used by the customer to remit payment (e.g., check, wire transfer, ACH, credit card, cash, lockbox).']
  posted_date date [note: 'The date on which the receipt was posted to the general ledger and became part of the official financial records.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the receipt was posted to the general ledger, supporting audit trail and transaction sequencing.']
  preparer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the staff member who prepared the receipt record, captured for audit and accountability purposes.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Program or project code associated with the receipt, used for grant accounting and program-level financial reporting.']
  receipt_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross amount received from the customer in the payment transaction, before any application to invoices or discounts.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment was received from the customer. This is the business event date for the cash receipt transaction.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the cash receipt, typically a sequential or formatted number assigned by the AR system for external reference and reconciliation.']
  receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cash receipt indicating its processing state within the AR workflow.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date on which the receipt was successfully reconciled with the bank statement.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the receipt has been reconciled with the bank statement and deposit records.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number provided by the customer or payment processor, such as a wire transfer confirmation number or transaction ID.']
  remittance_advice varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing remittance advice or payment notes provided by the customer indicating which invoices or charges the payment is intended to cover.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'The date on which the receipt was reversed or voided, if applicable.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this receipt has been reversed or voided in a subsequent transaction.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason code for why the receipt was reversed, such as NSF (non-sufficient funds), customer dispute, or data entry error.']
  unapplied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the receipt amount that remains unapplied and is held as a credit on the customer account, available for future application.']

  Note: 'Accounts receivable cash receipt record capturing payments received from external customers against AR invoices. Records receipt number, receipt date, payment method, bank deposit reference, amount received, discount allowed, customer reference, invoice references applied, unapplied cash amount, and bank reconciliation status. Sourced from Oracle PeopleSoft Financials AR module.'
}

  grant_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the grant account financial entity. Primary key for the grant account master record representing the financial accounting view of a sponsored project or externally funded award.']
  academic_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to curriculum.academic_program. Business justification: Research grants are tied to specific academic programs for IPEDS research expenditure reporting, indirect cost rate application, and accreditation standards (AACSB research activity, ABET). A higher-e']
  award_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.award_budget. Business justification: Grant accounts implement approved award budgets. Budget periods, IDC rates, cost categories from award_budget drive grant_account financial controls and spending authority. Required for budget vs actu']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Grant accounts generate specific compliance obligations (progress reports, financial reports, invention disclosures, training requirements for grant personnel). Grant compliance officers track these o']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Grant accounts in higher education are administered by specific departments/cost centers (e.g., the Chemistry Department administers an NSF grant). The grant_account table currently links to hr.org_un']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: In higher-education fund accounting, every grant account is associated with a specific restricted fund (the self-balancing accounting entity). The grant_account table has a fund_code STRING column tha']
  primary_grant_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the department that administratively owns this grant account.']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the faculty member serving as Principal Investigator for this grant.']
  proposal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.proposal. Business justification: Grant accounts originate from awarded proposals. Pre-award to post-award transition requires tracking which proposal generated each grant account for audit trail, compliance reporting, and institution']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Federal grants cite specific regulatory requirements (2 CFR 200, OMB Circulars, agency-specific terms). Grant administrators must track applicable regulations for allowability determinations, audit co']
  research_award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.research_award. Business justification: Core 1:1 relationship - every research award generates exactly one grant account for financial tracking. Award terms, budget periods, compliance requirements flow from award to grant account. Essentia']
  account_manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the research administrator or grants accountant managing this grant account.']
  account_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the research administrator or grants accountant assigned to manage the financial administration of this grant account.']
  account_setup_date date [note: 'Date when the grant account was established in the financial system and made available for transactions.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the grant account: pending (award received but not yet activated), active (open for transactions), suspended (temporarily frozen), closed (project ended and final reporting complete).']
  award_notice_date date [note: 'Date when the institution received official notification of the award from the sponsoring agency.']
  closeout_date date [note: 'Date when the grant account was officially closed after all financial reporting, final invoicing, and sponsor closeout requirements were completed.']
  cost_sharing_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total dollar value of institutional cost sharing or matching commitment required for this award. Null if no cost sharing is required.']
  cost_sharing_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the institution is required to contribute matching funds or in-kind resources as a condition of the award.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this grant account record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the award amount (e.g., USD for US Dollar, EUR for Euro, GBP for British Pound).']
  current_budget_period_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The funding amount authorized for the current budget period. Multi-year awards are typically funded incrementally by budget period.']
  current_budget_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the current budget period for multi-year awards that are funded incrementally.']
  current_budget_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the current budget period for multi-year awards that are funded incrementally.']
  fa_base_type varchar [note: 'The cost base to which the F&A rate is applied: MTDC (Modified Total Direct Costs - most common federal base), TDC (Total Direct Costs), salaries and wages only, or other sponsor-specific base.']
  fa_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The indirect cost rate (expressed as a percentage) applied to this grant to recover institutional overhead costs. Also known as IDC (Indirect Cost) rate. Negotiated with federal cognizant agency.']
  federal_award_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is a federal award subject to Uniform Guidance (2 CFR 200) and Single Audit requirements.']
  flow_through_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this grant includes subawards to other institutions (institution acts as pass-through entity).']
  grant_account_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique account number assigned to this sponsored project or grant for financial tracking and reporting. This is the business identifier used across financial systems and in communications with sponsors.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the person who last modified this grant account record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this grant account record was most recently updated.']
  last_transaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent financial transaction posted to this grant account.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional information, special terms and conditions, or administrative notes about this grant account.']
  prime_institution_name varchar [note: 'If this is a subaward, the name of the prime recipient institution that passes through funding to this institution. Null for prime awards.']
  prime_sponsor_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is a prime award (institution receives funding directly from sponsor) or a subaward (institution receives funding as a subrecipient through another institution).']
  project_code varchar [note: 'The project dimension code used in the financial system to track all transactions related to this specific grant or sponsored project.']
  sponsor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sponsoring organization: federal agency, state government, local government, nonprofit foundation, corporate/industry sponsor, or foreign government/entity.']

  Note: 'Sponsored project/grant account master record representing the financial accounting entity for an externally funded research or program award. Captures grant account number, award title, sponsoring agency (NSF, NIH, DOE, private foundation), award amount, project start and end dates, F&A (indirect cost) rate, F&A base type, PI name, administering department, CFDA number for federal awards, cost-sharing requirement, budget period, and account status. Distinct from the research domain\'s award record — this is the financial accounting view. Sourced from Oracle PeopleSoft Financials Projects/Grants module and Kuali Research.'
}

Table finance.fiscal_period {
  fiscal_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fiscal period record. Primary key.']
  adjustment_period_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is a special adjustment period (e.g., Period 13) used for year-end adjustments, accruals, and closing entries after the regular fiscal year periods have closed.']
  ap_close_date date [note: 'Actual calendar date when the Accounts Payable module was closed for this fiscal period. Null if the period is still open.']
  ap_status varchar [note: 'Current open/closed status of the period for Accounts Payable module transactions. Controls whether AP vouchers and payments can be posted to this period.']
  ar_close_date date [note: 'Actual calendar date when the Accounts Receivable module was closed for this fiscal period. Null if the period is still open.']
  ar_status varchar [note: 'Current open/closed status of the period for Accounts Receivable module transactions. Controls whether AR invoices and receipts can be posted to this period.']
  budget_carryforward_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether unspent budget balances from this period are eligible to carry forward to the next fiscal year. Institutional policy determines carryforward eligibility.']
  budget_close_date date [note: 'Actual calendar date when the Budget module was closed for this fiscal period. Null if the period is still open.']
  budget_status varchar [note: 'Current open/closed status of the period for budget transactions and budget checking. Controls whether budget entries and adjustments can be posted to this period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fiscal period record was first created in the system.']
  encumbrance_rollover_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether open encumbrances from this period are eligible to roll forward to the next fiscal year. Typically true for the final period of the fiscal year.']
  fa_rate_calculation_period_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this period is included in the base period for calculating Facilities and Administrative (F&A) indirect cost rates for sponsored research. True for periods used in F&A rate proposals.']
  fasb_reporting_period_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this period is included in FASB financial statement reporting. True for periods that contribute to annual FASB-compliant financial statements for private institutions.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four-digit fiscal year to which this period belongs (e.g., 2024). Aligns with institutional fiscal year cycle and GASB/FASB reporting requirements.']
  gasb_reporting_period_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this period is included in GASB financial statement reporting. True for periods that contribute to annual GASB-compliant financial statements for public institutions.']
  gl_close_date date [note: 'Actual calendar date when the General Ledger module was closed for this fiscal period. Null if the period is still open.']
  gl_closed_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who performed the General Ledger close for this fiscal period.']
  gl_status varchar [note: 'Current open/closed status of the period for General Ledger module transactions. Open periods accept new journal entries; closed periods are locked for posting.']
  ipeds_reporting_year varchar [note: 'The IPEDS reporting year to which this fiscal period maps for federal reporting purposes. IPEDS fiscal year typically runs July 1 through June 30 for most institutions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fiscal period record was last updated or modified.']
  nacubo_reporting_category varchar [note: 'NACUBO reporting category or classification to which this fiscal period maps for benchmarking and comparative financial reporting purposes.']
  period_description varchar [note: 'Additional descriptive text or notes about the fiscal period, including any special circumstances, adjustments, or institutional context relevant to this period.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the fiscal period. Defines the end of the accounting period for transaction posting and reporting.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the fiscal period (e.g., \'July 2024\', \'Period 01 FY2024\', \'Year-End Adjustment Period\').']
  period_number varchar [note: 'Sequential numeric identifier for the period within the fiscal year (e.g., 1 through 12 for monthly periods, or 1 through 13 for institutions with a 13th adjustment period).']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the fiscal period. Defines the beginning of the accounting period for transaction posting and reporting.']
  period_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the fiscal period record. Active periods are current or future; inactive periods are historical but accessible; archived periods are retained for compliance but not actively used.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fiscal period type. Regular periods are standard monthly/quarterly periods; adjustment periods are used for year-end entries; opening/closing periods mark fiscal year boundaries.']
  purchasing_close_date date [note: 'Actual calendar date when the Purchasing module was closed for this fiscal period. Null if the period is still open.']
  purchasing_status varchar [note: 'Current open/closed status of the period for Purchasing module transactions. Controls whether purchase orders and requisitions can be posted to this period.']
  quarter_number varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter to which this period belongs within the fiscal year (1, 2, 3, or 4). Used for quarterly financial reporting and analysis.']
  year_end_close_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this period is designated as the year-end closing period for the fiscal year. True if this is the final period or adjustment period for fiscal year-end close activities.']

  Note: 'Institutional fiscal calendar reference data defining accounting periods, fiscal years, and key financial close dates. Captures fiscal year, fiscal period number, period name, period start date, period end date, open/closed status for each module (GL, AP, AR, purchasing), year-end close indicator, IPEDS reporting year alignment, and GASB reporting period flag. Serves as the authoritative fiscal calendar for all financial transactions and reporting across Oracle PeopleSoft Financials modules.'
}

Table finance.bank_account {
  bank_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the institutional bank account record. Primary key for the bank account master data.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Bank accounts are assigned to organizational cost centers for budget and responsibility tracking. Adding cost_center_id FK allows joining to cost_center master for cost_center_name, hierarchy, manager']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Bank accounts are assigned to funds (self-balancing accounting entities) for fund accounting. Adding fund_id FK allows joining to finance_fund master for fund_name, fund_type, net_asset_classification']
  gl_cash_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger_account. Business justification: Bank accounts map to GL cash accounts for general ledger integration. Adding gl_cash_ledger_account_id FK allows joining to ledger_account master for account_name, account_type, hierarchy, and classif']
  account_closed_date date [note: 'The date when the bank account was officially closed. Null for active accounts. Used for historical record-keeping and audit trail purposes.']
  account_manager_email varchar [note: 'The email address of the institutional employee responsible for managing this bank account. Used for notifications, workflow routing, and audit inquiries.']
  account_manager_name varchar [note: 'The name of the institutional employee responsible for managing this bank account, including reconciliation, transaction monitoring, and relationship coordination.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'The descriptive name or title assigned to the bank account for internal identification and reporting purposes (e.g., General Operating Account, Payroll Disbursement Account, Restricted Grant Account).']
  account_number varchar [note: 'The full bank account number assigned by the financial institution. Typically masked in reporting for security purposes. Used for deposits, disbursements, and reconciliation.']
  account_number_masked varchar [note: 'Partially masked version of the bank account number for display purposes (e.g., ****1234). Used in reports and user interfaces to protect sensitive financial data while maintaining reference capability.']
  account_opened_date date [note: 'The date when the bank account was originally opened with the financial institution. Used for account history tracking and relationship management.']
  account_purpose varchar [note: 'The primary business purpose or functional use of the bank account within institutional operations. Aligns with fund accounting and treasury management practices. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: operating|payroll|disbursement|deposit|grant|endowment|auxiliary|agency|loan|construction — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bank account. Determines whether the account is available for transactions, reconciliation, and cash management operations.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bank account based on its financial instrument type. Determines transaction capabilities, interest accrual, and liquidity characteristics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: checking|savings|money_market|sweep|payroll|restricted|investment|escrow — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ach_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is enabled for ACH transactions (direct deposits, electronic payments). Critical for payroll, vendor payments, and student refunds.']
  authorized_signatories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of names or identifiers of individuals authorized to sign checks, initiate wire transfers, or approve transactions on this account. Critical for internal controls and segregation of duties.']
  available_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The available balance in the account after accounting for outstanding checks, pending deposits, and holds. Represents funds available for immediate disbursement.']
  bank_branch_address varchar [note: 'The physical street address of the bank branch where the account is held. Used for correspondence and audit documentation.']
  bank_branch_name varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the specific bank branch where the account is maintained. Used for relationship management and service coordination.']
  bank_contact_email varchar [note: 'The email address of the bank relationship manager or account representative for electronic communication regarding account matters.']
  bank_contact_name varchar [note: 'The name of the primary relationship manager or account representative at the financial institution responsible for this account.']
  bank_contact_phone varchar [note: 'The telephone number of the bank relationship manager or account representative for account inquiries and issue resolution.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'The legal name of the financial institution where the account is held (e.g., Bank of America, Wells Fargo, PNC Bank).']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'The nine-digit American Bankers Association (ABA) routing transit number that identifies the financial institution for electronic funds transfers, wire transfers, and ACH transactions.']
  collateral_type varchar [note: 'The type of collateral pledged by the financial institution to secure deposits (e.g., US Treasury securities, agency securities, municipal bonds). Used for risk assessment and compliance monitoring.']
  collateral_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current market value of collateral pledged by the financial institution to secure deposits. Must meet or exceed required collateralization percentage per institutional policy.']
  collateralization_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the financial institution is required to provide collateral for deposits exceeding FDIC insurance limits. Common for large public fund deposits per state law.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this bank account record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the denomination of the bank account (e.g., USD for US Dollar, EUR for Euro, GBP for British Pound). Most institutional accounts are USD.']
  current_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current available balance in the bank account as of the last reconciliation or bank statement. Used for cash management and liquidity monitoring.']
  dual_signature_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'The transaction amount threshold above which dual signatures are required for disbursements. Used to enforce internal control policies and mitigate fraud risk.']
  fdic_coverage_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum amount of FDIC insurance coverage applicable to this account (typically $250,000 per depositor per institution). Used for risk assessment and collateralization monitoring.']
  fdic_insured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bank account is covered by FDIC insurance up to the statutory limit. Critical for risk management and investment policy compliance.']
  interest_accrual_method varchar [note: 'The frequency and method by which interest is calculated and credited to the account. Determines timing of interest income recognition.']
  interest_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The annual interest rate percentage applied to the account balance for interest-bearing accounts. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0250 for 2.50%).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this bank account record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  last_reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date when the most recent bank reconciliation was completed for this account. Used to monitor compliance with reconciliation policies and identify overdue reconciliations.']
  minimum_balance_required decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum balance that must be maintained in the account to avoid fees or meet contractual requirements with the financial institution.']
  next_reconciliation_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the next bank reconciliation must be completed based on the reconciliation frequency policy. Used for workflow management and compliance monitoring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, special instructions, or contextual information about the bank account. Used for documentation of unique account characteristics or restrictions.']
  online_banking_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether online banking access is enabled for this account. Determines availability of electronic statements, transaction history, and real-time balance inquiries.']
  positive_pay_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positive pay fraud prevention service is enabled for this account. Matches issued checks against presented checks to prevent fraudulent payments.']
  reconciliation_frequency varchar [note: 'The required frequency for performing bank reconciliation procedures to match institutional records with bank statements. Critical for internal controls and audit compliance.']
  signatory_requirement varchar [note: 'The number of authorized signatures required to approve transactions above specified thresholds. Supports internal control policies and fraud prevention.']
  wire_transfer_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is enabled for domestic and international wire transfers. Used for large-value payments, grant disbursements, and international transactions.']

  Note: 'Institutional bank account master record for all depository and disbursement accounts maintained by the institution. Captures bank name, bank routing number, account number (masked), account type (checking, money market, sweep, payroll, restricted), account purpose, currency, bank contact, authorized signatories, reconciliation frequency, and active status. Supports bank reconciliation, cash management, and treasury operations in PeopleSoft Financials.'
}

Table finance.fixed_asset {
  fixed_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fixed asset record. Primary key for institutional capitalized property, plant, and equipment tracking.']
  acquisition_ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: Fixed assets are typically acquired through vendor invoices processed via accounts payable. While fixed_asset already has purchase_order_id linking to the PO, the AP invoice is the actual payment docu']
  acquisition_fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Fixed assets are placed in service during a specific fiscal period, which determines the start of depreciation calculations, GASB reporting period, and capitalization timing. The fixed_asset table has']
  custodial_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Fixed assets are assigned to custodial cost centers for responsibility tracking. Adding custodial_cost_center_id FK allows joining to cost_center master for cost_center_name, hierarchy, manager, and b']
  donor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual or organization that donated funds or the asset itself. Used for stewardship reporting and recognition when funding source is gift.']
  facility_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.asset. Business justification: The finance fixed asset register and facilities asset inventory must reconcile for GASB 34/35 capital asset reporting and annual physical inventory audits. Higher-ed controllers and facilities directo']
  facility_building_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.building. Business justification: Fixed assets need structured building location for physical inventory audits, insurance valuation, capital planning, and asset disposal processes. The existing building_code text field should be repla']
  facility_room_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.room. Business justification: Equipment and movable assets require room-level location tracking for annual physical inventory audits, asset transfers, theft prevention, and custodial responsibility. Room-level precision is essenti']
  grant_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sponsored research grant or award that funded the asset acquisition. Required for federal and sponsor compliance reporting when funding source is grant.']
  ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger_account. Business justification: Fixed assets must be associated with a GL ledger account (e.g., asset account code for PP&E, accumulated depreciation account). This FK enables proper capitalization accounting, GASB 34/35 reporting, ']
  originating_capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.capital_project. Business justification: Fixed assets created through capital construction or renovation projects must reference the originating project for capitalization closeout, project-to-asset transfer reporting, and GASB 34 infrastruc']
  policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Capitalization policies define thresholds, useful life tables, depreciation methods, and disposal procedures. Controllers enforce policy for GASB 34/35 compliance and financial statement accuracy. Pol']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.purchase_order. Business justification: Fixed assets are acquired via purchase orders. Adding purchase_order_id FK allows joining to purchase_order for PO details, vendor, dates, and approval information. Removes redundant purchase_order_nu']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Federally-funded equipment is subject to specific regulatory requirements (2 CFR 200.313 equipment management, export control regulations for research equipment). Fixed asset managers must track which']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.vendor. Business justification: Fixed assets are acquired from vendors. Adding vendor_id FK allows joining to vendor master for legal_name, contact information, payment terms, and vendor classification attributes. Removes redundant ']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total depreciation expense recognized to date since acquisition. Contra-asset account that reduces the gross book value to calculate net book value.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total capitalized cost of the asset including purchase price, freight, installation, and other costs necessary to place the asset into service. Historical cost basis for depreciation.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the institution obtained legal ownership or placed the asset into service, whichever is earlier. Used as the starting point for depreciation calculation.']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the fixed asset type for depreciation, reporting, and GASB 34 compliance. Determines capitalization threshold and useful life assignment.']
  asset_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the fixed asset including make, model, specifications, and distinguishing characteristics for identification and inventory purposes.']
  asset_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the asset indicating its operational state and availability for use. Drives depreciation continuation and inventory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|in_storage|under_repair|retired|disposed|missing|transferred — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  asset_subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing granular detail within the primary asset category (e.g., laboratory equipment, instructional technology, administrative software, rare books).']
  asset_tag_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique identifier affixed to physical asset for inventory control and tracking. Barcode or RFID tag number used in physical audits.']
  capitalization_threshold_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset acquisition cost met or exceeded the institutional capitalization threshold policy. Determines if asset is capitalized or expensed.']
  condition_assessment varchar [note: 'Qualitative evaluation of the asset physical condition and operational functionality recorded during physical inventory or maintenance inspection.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the fixed asset record was first created in the asset management system. Audit trail for data governance and compliance.']
  custodian_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the assigned custodian for asset verification notifications and inventory audit communications.']
  custodian_name varchar [note: 'Name of the faculty or staff member assigned primary responsibility for the asset. Contact person for physical verification and condition assessment.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to allocate the cost of the asset over its useful life. Straight-line is standard for higher education institutions per GASB guidance.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date the asset was removed from service through sale, donation, trade-in, or retirement. Triggers final depreciation calculation and gain/loss recognition.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the asset was removed from institutional inventory. Required for financial reporting and compliance with property disposition regulations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sale|donation|trade_in|scrap|theft|casualty_loss|transfer — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  disposal_proceeds decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cash or fair market value received from the disposal of the asset. Used to calculate gain or loss on disposal for financial statement reporting.']
  federal_property_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset was acquired with federal funds and remains subject to federal property management requirements and disposition approval per OMB Uniform Guidance.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Primary source of funds used to acquire the asset. Critical for grant compliance, donor stewardship, and facilities and administrative (F&A) cost rate calculations.']
  gasb_classification varchar [note: 'GASB Statement 34 capital asset classification code for external financial reporting. Maps institutional asset categories to standardized government reporting requirements.']
  in_service_date date [note: 'Date the asset was placed into active operational use. May differ from acquisition date for assets requiring installation or construction completion.']
  insurance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset requires specific insurance coverage beyond general institutional property insurance due to high value, portability, or specialized nature.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the fixed asset record was most recently updated. Tracks data currency and supports change audit requirements.']
  last_physical_inventory_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical verification of the asset location and condition. Required for audit compliance and inventory accuracy certification.']
  lease_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset is leased rather than owned. Triggers different accounting treatment under GASB 87 lease accounting standards.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured or produced the asset. Used for warranty claims, maintenance contracts, and vendor management.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model or part number identifying the specific product configuration. Essential for replacement parts ordering and technical support.']
  net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current carrying value of the asset calculated as acquisition cost minus accumulated depreciation. Represents the undepreciated portion reported on the balance sheet.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional information about the asset including special handling requirements, historical significance, donor restrictions, or unique characteristics.']
  salvage_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated residual value of the asset at the end of its useful life. Subtracted from acquisition cost to determine the depreciable base.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number for equipment identification, warranty tracking, and theft recovery. Critical for high-value technology and scientific equipment.']
  title_holder varchar [note: 'Legal owner of the asset. May differ from custodian for federally-owned equipment, leased assets, or property with donor restrictions on title transfer.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Expected service life of the asset in years as determined by institutional policy and GASB guidelines. Used to calculate annual depreciation expense.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer or vendor warranty coverage ends. Used to plan maintenance contract purchases and budget for repair costs.']

  Note: 'Institutional fixed asset master record for capitalized property, plant, and equipment. Captures asset tag number, asset description, asset category (land, buildings, equipment, library collections, software, construction in progress), acquisition date, acquisition cost, funding source (institutional, grant, gift), useful life, depreciation method (straight-line), accumulated depreciation, net book value, physical location (building/room), custodial department, disposal date, and GASB 34 capital asset classification. Sourced from Oracle PeopleSoft Financials Asset Management module.'
}

Table finance.ap_payment_application {
  ap_payment_application_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the ap_payment_application association']
  ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor invoice being settled by this payment application.']
  ap_payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the accounts payable payment disbursement record that is being applied.']
  amount_applied decimal [note: 'The portion of the payment amount applied to this specific invoice. Belongs to the application record because a single payment may be split across multiple invoices and a single invoice may receive partial payments from multiple disbursements.']
  application_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this payment application record (e.g., applied, reversed, pending, voided). Belongs to the application record because a payment application can be reversed independently of the payment or invoice status.']
  discount_taken_amount decimal [note: 'The early payment discount amount captured at the time of this specific payment application against this invoice. Belongs to the application record because the discount is realized per invoice per payment event, not at the payment or invoice level alone.']
  invoice_count varchar [note: 'The number of vendor invoices paid by this single payment disbursement. Supports batch payment analysis. [Moved from ap_payment: invoice_count on ap_payment is a derived aggregate (count of invoices paid by this payment) that becomes redundant and computable once the ap_payment_application association table exists. It does not belong on the payment record itself — it is a property of the payment-to-invoice relationship set, derivable via COUNT(*) on ap_payment_application grouped by ap_payment_id.]']
  payment_application_date date [note: 'The date on which this payment was applied to this invoice in the AP system. Belongs to the application record as it records when the settlement event occurred for this specific payment-invoice pairing.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Payment Application event between ap_payment and ap_invoice. It captures the operational record of how a specific payment disbursement is applied against a specific vendor invoice, including the amount applied, any discount taken at application time, the application date, and the application status. Each record links one ap_payment to one ap_invoice and is the authoritative source for vendor ledger reconciliation, audit compliance, and cash flow reporting in the institutional accounts payable process.. Existence Justification: In accounts payable operations, a single payment disbursement (ap_payment) routinely settles multiple vendor invoices simultaneously — this is the standard \'payment run\' process in ERP systems like Oracle PeopleSoft. Conversely, a single invoice can be partially paid across multiple payment disbursements (partial payments, installment arrangements, or split payment runs). The business actively manages this payment application process, tracking exactly how much of each payment was applied to each invoice, with its own attributes (amount_applied, discount_taken, application_date, status) that belong to neither the payment nor the invoice alone.'
}

Table finance.ar_receipt_application {
  ar_receipt_application_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the ar_receipt_application association']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the AR invoice being satisfied by this application. Identifies which outstanding invoice is being reduced by this cash application.']
  ar_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the cash receipt being applied. Identifies which payment transaction is the source of funds for this application.']
  application_date date [note: 'The date on which this specific receipt-to-invoice application was posted in the AR system. May differ from the receipt date (when payment was received) and the invoice date. Required for period-accurate AR aging and sponsored program reporting.']
  application_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this specific receipt-to-invoice application record. Tracks whether the application is fully posted, partially applied, or has been reversed (e.g., due to a returned check or correction). Belongs to the application event, not to the receipt or invoice independently.']
  applied_amount decimal [note: 'The portion of the receipt amount applied to this specific invoice in this application record. Belongs to the application event, not to the receipt total or the invoice balance alone. Summing applied_amount across all applications for a receipt yields the receipt\'s total applied amount; summing across all applications for an invoice yields amount_paid.']
  discount_amount decimal [note: 'Discount amount granted on this specific receipt-to-invoice application (e.g., early payment discount). Distinct from the receipt-level discount_amount aggregate and the invoice-level discount_amount. Belongs to the application event.']
  invoice_references varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or concatenated string of invoice numbers to which this receipt has been applied, for quick reference and reporting. [Moved from ar_receipt: This STRING column is a denormalized workaround storing multiple invoice numbers as a comma-separated list. Its data is fully replaced by the ar_receipt_application association table, where each receipt-to-invoice link is a proper relational row. Removing this column eliminates the normalization violation and enables proper FK-based querying, reporting, and audit tracing.]']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Cash Application event between ar_receipt and ar_invoice. It captures the operational record of how a specific cash receipt is applied — in full or in part — against a specific AR invoice, including the amount applied, any discount granted, the application date, and the lifecycle status of the application. Each record links one ar_receipt to one ar_invoice and carries attributes that exist only in the context of this receipt-to-invoice application. Required for GASB compliance, sponsored program financial reporting, and AR aging accuracy.. Existence Justification: In higher-education AR operations, a single cash receipt (e.g., a lump-sum payment from a government sponsor) is routinely applied across multiple outstanding invoices simultaneously, and a single invoice can receive partial payments from multiple separate receipts over its lifecycle. This is the standard \'cash application\' or \'receipt application\' process in Oracle PeopleSoft AR. The relationship is actively managed by AR staff who create, update, and close application records — it is not derivable from joins alone.'
}

Table facility.building {
  building_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the building. Primary key for the building master record.']
  campus_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the campus on which this building is located. Links to the campus master record.']
  responsible_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.org_unit. Business justification: FICM (Facilities Inventory and Classification Manual) reporting and utilities/maintenance budget allocation require identifying the primary administrative org unit responsible for each building. Highe']
  ada_compliance_date date [note: 'Date on which the building was certified as ADA compliant or most recent ADA compliance assessment date.']
  ada_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the building meets ADA accessibility requirements including accessible entrances, elevators, restrooms, and pathways.']
  annual_operating_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total annual operating cost for the building including utilities, maintenance, custodial services, and routine repairs.']
  assignable_square_feet decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net assignable square footage available for occupancy and functional use, excluding circulation, mechanical, and structural areas. Critical for Facilities and Administrative (F&A) cost rate calculations reported to federal sponsors.']
  automation_system varchar [note: 'Name or type of the building automation system used to control HVAC, lighting, and other building systems.']
  bike_parking_spaces varchar [note: 'Number of bicycle parking spaces available at or near the building.']
  building_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to uniquely identify the building in campus systems and signage. This is the externally-known business identifier used in room numbering, wayfinding, and operational systems.']
  building_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the building, often honoring a donor, historical figure, or reflecting the building\'s primary function.']
  building_type varchar [note: 'Primary functional classification of the building indicating its predominant use category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: academic|residential|administrative|research|athletic|library|student_services|dining|parking|utility — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  city varchar [note: 'City in which the building is located.']
  construction_year varchar [note: 'Year in which the building was originally constructed and completed.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the building location.']
  deferred_maintenance_backlog decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated dollar value of accumulated deferred maintenance and capital renewal needs for the building.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this building record became effective in the system, typically the date the building was added to the institutional inventory.']
  emergency_generator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the building is equipped with an emergency backup generator for power continuity during outages.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date on which this building record was retired or decommissioned. Null for active buildings.']
  energy_star_score varchar [note: 'EPA Energy Star portfolio manager score (1-100) indicating the building\'s energy performance relative to similar buildings nationwide.']
