  floor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for floor']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the building that contains this floor. Links floor to its parent building structure.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Primary academic or administrative department assigned to or responsible for the floor. Used for space allocation and chargeback.']
  parent_floor_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on floor (parent_floor_id)']
  ada_accessible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the floor is fully accessible to individuals with disabilities per ADA requirements. Includes elevator access, accessible restrooms, and compliant pathways.']
  ceiling_finish_type varchar [note: 'Primary ceiling finish or construction type on the floor. Affects acoustics, lighting, and maintenance requirements.']
  ceiling_height_feet decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average ceiling height on the floor measured in feet. Important for space utilization, HVAC design, and ADA compliance.']
  circulation_area_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Square footage dedicated to corridors, hallways, lobbies, and other circulation spaces on the floor. Used for space efficiency analysis.']
  construction_year varchar [note: 'Year the floor was originally constructed. Used for capital planning, renovation scheduling, and historical facility records.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center code for charging maintenance, utilities, and facility costs associated with the floor.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the floor record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit purposes.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the current floor configuration and attributes became effective. Used for historical space tracking and audit trails.']
  elevation_feet decimal(10,2) [note: 'Vertical elevation of the floor level above a reference datum (typically ground level or building entrance). Used for accessibility planning and emergency response.']
  emergency_exit_count varchar [note: 'Number of emergency exits available on the floor. Used for life safety compliance and emergency evacuation planning.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current floor record expires or is superseded. Null for currently active records. Supports historical space analysis.']
  finish_type varchar [note: 'Primary flooring material or finish type on the floor. Impacts maintenance costs, cleaning schedules, and replacement planning.']
  fire_zone varchar [note: 'Fire safety zone designation for the floor. Used for fire alarm system configuration, emergency response planning, and code compliance.']
  floor_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the floor within the institution\'s facility coding system. Used for space accounting and reporting.']
  floor_name varchar [note: 'Common or official name of the floor (e.g., Main Floor, Engineering Wing, Administrative Level). Used for wayfinding and communication.']
  floor_number varchar [note: 'The floor number or level designation within the building (e.g., 1, 2, B1 for basement, M for mezzanine, G for ground). May include alphanumeric codes for special levels.']
  floor_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the floor. Indicates availability for space assignment and occupancy.']
  floor_type varchar [note: 'Primary functional classification of the floor based on predominant use. Determines space allocation policies and cost recovery rates.']
  gross_area_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total gross square footage of the floor measured from exterior wall to exterior wall. Used for facility cost allocation and space planning.']
  hvac_zone varchar [note: 'HVAC control zone identifier for the floor. Used for energy management, temperature control, and utility cost allocation.']
  last_renovation_year varchar [note: 'Year of the most recent major renovation or remodeling of the floor. Used for deferred maintenance planning and lifecycle analysis.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the floor record. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  maintenance_zone varchar [note: 'Facilities maintenance zone assignment for work order routing and custodial service scheduling.']
  mechanical_area_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Square footage occupied by mechanical systems, HVAC equipment, electrical rooms, and building service areas on the floor.']
  natural_light_availability varchar [note: 'Assessment of natural daylight availability on the floor. Impacts energy efficiency, occupant well-being, and space desirability.']
  net_assignable_area_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net assignable square footage available for functional use, excluding circulation, mechanical, and structural spaces. Used for Facilities and Administrative (F&A) cost rate calculations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional information about the floor, including special characteristics, restrictions, or historical context.']
  occupancy_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of occupants permitted on the floor based on building code, fire safety regulations, and egress capacity. Used for emergency planning.']
  restroom_count varchar [note: 'Total number of restroom facilities on the floor. Used for occupancy planning and ADA compliance verification.']
  room_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual rooms or spaces on the floor. Used for space inventory and utilization tracking.']
  security_zone varchar [note: 'Security access control zone designation. Determines access card permissions, surveillance coverage, and security protocols.']
  space_use_category varchar [note: 'Facilities and Administrative (F&A) cost rate space use category code. Used for federal indirect cost rate calculations and sponsored research administration.']
  usable_area_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Usable square footage including assignable space plus circulation areas on the floor. Excludes only major vertical penetrations and building service areas.']
  wireless_coverage boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the floor has complete wireless network coverage. Important for academic technology planning and student services.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for floor. Referenced by floor_id.'
}

Table advancement.alumnus {
  alumnus_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the alumnus record. Primary key for the alumnus entity. This is the system-generated surrogate key that uniquely identifies each individual who has graduated from or attended the institution.']
  academic_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to curriculum.academic_program. Business justification: Alumni segmentation, reunion planning, program-specific fundraising appeals, and IPEDS alumni outcome reporting all require linking alumni to their graduating academic program. Higher-ed advancement o']
  cip_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to curriculum.cip_code. Business justification: Alumni profile stores primary degree CIP for segmentation, STEM alumni tracking, and targeted solicitation campaigns. Proper FK enables accurate STEM designation queries for NSF alumni surveys and OPT']
  degree_conferral_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to student.degree_conferral. Business justification: Alumni records are created from degree conferral events. Advancement staff use this link for alumni segmentation by degree level/major, stewardship reporting, and reunion class assignment. A higher-ed']
  graduating_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.org_unit. Business justification: Alumni segmentation by graduating college/department drives targeted fundraising campaigns, reunion planning, and IPEDS alumni reporting. Higher-ed advancement offices routinely segment alumni by acad']
  graduation_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.term. Business justification: Reunion planning and cohort giving reports require linking alumni to their graduation term record (census date, commencement date, term type). graduation_term_code is a denormalized plain attribute; r']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Cross-reference to the original student identifier from the Student Information System (SIS). Links the alumnus record back to their student record for historical academic data retrieval. This is the business key that was assigned during enrollment.']
  alternate_email varchar [note: 'A secondary email address for the alumnus. Used as a backup contact method when the primary email is undeliverable or for work vs personal email segmentation.']
  alumni_status varchar [note: 'The current engagement status of the alumnus record. Active indicates the alumnus is reachable and engaged; Deceased indicates confirmed death; Lost indicates contact information is outdated; Do Not Contact indicates explicit request to cease communications.']
  cumulative_gpa decimal(4,3) [note: 'The cumulative GPA earned by the alumnus at the time of graduation. Used for honors society identification, scholarship donor reporting, and academic achievement recognition. Typically on a 4.0 scale.']
  current_employer varchar [note: 'The name of the organization where the alumnus is currently employed. Used for career outcomes tracking, employer engagement, and prospect research for major gift fundraising.']
  current_job_title varchar [note: 'The current professional title or position of the alumnus. Used for career outcomes reporting, networking events, and wealth screening for fundraising.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'The date of birth of the alumnus. Used for age-based segmentation, reunion year calculations, and identity verification. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  deceased_date date [note: 'The date on which the alumnus passed away, if known. Used for memorial records and in memoriam recognition. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  deceased_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the alumnus is deceased. True if confirmed deceased, False otherwise. Used to suppress communications and update alumni records appropriately.']
  do_not_contact_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating the alumnus has requested no contact from the institution. True if do-not-contact request is active, False otherwise. Suppresses all outreach including fundraising, events, and communications.']
  do_not_solicit_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating the alumnus has requested no fundraising solicitations. True if do-not-solicit request is active, False otherwise. Suppresses fundraising appeals but allows other alumni communications.']
  ethnicity varchar [note: 'The self-reported ethnicity or race of the alumnus. Used for diversity reporting, IPEDS compliance, and targeted outreach to underrepresented alumni populations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Hispanic or Latino|American Indian or Alaska Native|Asian|Black or African American|Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander|White|Two or More Races|Unknown — promote to reference product]']
  ferpa_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the alumnus has provided consent to share directory information under FERPA regulations. True if consent given, False if restricted. Controls publication of alumni information in directories and public listings.']
  gender varchar [note: 'The self-identified gender of the alumnus. Used for demographic reporting and diversity analytics. Supports inclusive gender identity options.']
  legal_first_name varchar [note: 'The legal first name of the alumnus as recorded in official institutional records and on the degree conferred. Used for official transcripts, diplomas, and legal correspondence.']
  legal_last_name varchar [note: 'The legal last name (surname/family name) of the alumnus as recorded in official institutional records and on the degree conferred. Used for official transcripts, diplomas, and legal correspondence.']
  legal_middle_name varchar [note: 'The legal middle name or initial of the alumnus as recorded in official institutional records. May be null if no middle name was provided.']
  linkedin_profile_url varchar [note: 'The URL to the alumnus LinkedIn profile. Used for professional networking, career outcomes verification, and prospect research.']
  maiden_name varchar [note: 'The birth surname of the alumnus, typically used to track alumni who have changed their last name after graduation. Critical for alumni search and reunion planning.']
  mailing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'The first line of the alumnus mailing address (street number and name, apartment/unit number). Used for direct mail campaigns, alumni magazine distribution, and event invitations.']
  mailing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'The second line of the alumnus mailing address (additional address details such as suite, building, or care-of information). Optional field.']
  mailing_city varchar [note: 'The city of the alumnus mailing address. Used for geographic segmentation of alumni populations and regional event planning.']
  mailing_country_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO country code of the alumnus mailing address. Used for international alumni segmentation and global engagement strategies.']
  mailing_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal code or ZIP code of the alumnus mailing address. Used for mail sorting, geographic analysis, and targeted regional campaigns.']
  mailing_state_province varchar [note: 'The state or province of the alumnus mailing address. Used for regional alumni chapter organization and state-level reporting.']
  mobile_phone varchar [note: 'The mobile/cell phone number of the alumnus. Used for SMS communications, text-to-give campaigns, and mobile-first engagement strategies.']
  preferred_first_name varchar [note: 'The first name the alumnus prefers to be called in informal communications, alumni events, and publications. May differ from legal name. Used for personalized engagement and respecting individual identity preferences.']
  preferred_last_name varchar [note: 'The last name the alumnus prefers to be used in informal communications and alumni engagement activities. Often reflects name changes due to marriage or personal preference.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'The primary email address for contacting the alumnus. Used for alumni communications, event invitations, fundraising campaigns, and institutional updates. This is the preferred digital contact channel.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'The primary telephone number for contacting the alumnus. Used for phonathon campaigns, event reminders, and urgent communications.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this alumnus record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  record_source varchar [note: 'The system or process that created or last significantly updated this alumnus record. Used for data lineage tracking and data quality auditing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SIS|CRM|Manual Entry|Data Import|Alumni Survey|Third Party|Merge — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this alumnus record was last modified. Used for audit trail, data freshness assessment, and change tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Master record for every individual who has graduated from or attended the institution. Serves as the SSOT for alumni identity, capturing legal name, preferred name, graduation year, degree(s) earned, primary major/CIP code, student ID cross-reference, demographic data, FERPA consent flags, deceased indicator, and record source. This is the foundational entity for all alumni relations, advancement, and career services activities. Supports IPEDS graduation rate reporting and alumni participation rate calculations.'
}

Table advancement.alumni_contact {
  alumni_contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the alumni contact record. Primary key.']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alumnus this contact record belongs to. Links to the alumni master record.']
  address_verification_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the address has been verified through USPS or other address validation services.']
  contact_frequency_preference varchar [note: 'How often the alumnus wishes to receive communications from the institution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this contact record was first created in the system.']
  do_not_call_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates the alumnus has opted out of telephone communications.']
  do_not_contact_flag boolean [note: 'Global flag indicating the alumnus has requested no contact from the institution across all channels.']
  do_not_email_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates the alumnus has opted out of email communications.']
  do_not_mail_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates the alumnus has opted out of postal mail communications.']
  do_not_sms_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates the alumnus has opted out of SMS text message communications.']
  email_consent_source varchar [note: 'The channel or method through which email consent was obtained.']
  email_consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the alumnus provided consent for email communications.']
  home_address_line1 varchar [note: 'The first line of the alumnus home mailing address (street number and name).']
  home_address_line2 varchar [note: 'The second line of the alumnus home mailing address (apartment, suite, unit number).']
  home_city varchar [note: 'The city of the alumnus home address.']
  home_country_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO country code of the alumnus home address.']
  home_phone varchar [note: 'The alumnus home telephone number for voice contact.']
  home_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal or ZIP code of the alumnus home address.']
  home_state_province varchar [note: 'The state or province of the alumnus home address.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this contact record was last updated.']
  linkedin_profile_url varchar [note: 'The URL to the alumnus LinkedIn profile for professional networking and career tracking.']
  mailing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'The first line of the alumnus preferred mailing address if different from home address.']
  mailing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'The second line of the alumnus preferred mailing address.']
  mailing_city varchar [note: 'The city of the alumnus preferred mailing address.']
  mailing_country_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO country code of the alumnus preferred mailing address.']
  mailing_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal or ZIP code of the alumnus preferred mailing address.']
  mailing_state_province varchar [note: 'The state or province of the alumnus preferred mailing address.']
  mobile_phone varchar [note: 'The alumnus mobile telephone number for voice and SMS contact.']
  ncoa_update_date date [note: 'The date when the address was last updated through the USPS National Change of Address database.']
  opt_in_career_news boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus has opted in to receive career-related news and opportunities.']
  opt_in_event_invitations boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus has opted in to receive invitations to alumni events.']
  opt_in_fundraising_appeals boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus has opted in to receive fundraising and donation appeals.']
  opt_in_institutional_news boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus has opted in to receive general institutional news and updates.']
  phone_consent_source varchar [note: 'The channel or method through which phone consent was obtained.']
  phone_consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the alumnus provided consent for phone communications.']
  preferred_contact_method varchar [note: 'The alumnus preferred method for receiving communications from the institution.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'The alumnus preferred language for communications using ISO 639-2 three-letter codes (ENG=English, SPA=Spanish, FRA=French, DEU=German, ZHO=Chinese, JPN=Japanese).']
  preferred_name varchar [note: 'The name the alumnus prefers to be addressed by in communications and events.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'The primary email address for alumni communications. Used as the default contact channel for digital outreach.']
  seasonal_address_line1 varchar [note: 'The first line of a seasonal or temporary address (e.g., winter residence).']
  seasonal_address_line2 varchar [note: 'The second line of a seasonal or temporary address.']
  seasonal_city varchar [note: 'The city of the seasonal or temporary address.']
  seasonal_country_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO country code of the seasonal or temporary address.']
  seasonal_end_date date [note: 'The date when the seasonal address becomes inactive each year.']
  seasonal_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal or ZIP code of the seasonal or temporary address.']
  seasonal_start_date date [note: 'The date when the seasonal address becomes active each year.']
  seasonal_state_province varchar [note: 'The state or province of the seasonal or temporary address.']
  work_phone varchar [note: 'The alumnus work or business telephone number.']

  Note: 'Stores all contact information and explicit communication preferences for each alumnus. Contact data includes addresses (home, mailing, seasonal, international), email addresses (personal, professional), phone numbers, LinkedIn profile URL, address verification status, and NCOA update dates. Communication preferences include preferred language, preferred contact method (email, postal mail, phone, SMS), frequency preferences (weekly, monthly, annual), topic opt-ins (career news, event invitations, fundraising appeals, institutional news), global do-not-contact flag, opt-in/opt-out flags per channel, consent timestamps, and consent source (web form, phone, event). Supports multi-address scenarios, FERPA-compliant outreach, CAN-SPAM/GDPR compliance, and international alumni communications.'
}

Table advancement.engagement_activity {
  engagement_activity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each engagement activity record. Primary key for the engagement activity entity.']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alumnus who participated in this engagement activity. Links to the alumni master record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fundraising or engagement campaign associated with this activity. Nullable for activities not tied to a specific campaign.']
  campus_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studentlife.campus_event. Business justification: Advancement officers record alumni engagement activities when alumni attend campus events (not just alumni-specific events). Linking engagement_activity to campus_event enables moves management scorin']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific event record if this activity represents attendance at or participation in a scheduled event. Nullable for non-event activities.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Engagement activities occur in specific fiscal periods for donor relations tracking and reporting. Creating engagement_activity.fiscal_period_id to link to finance.fiscal_period for period-based repor']
  major_gift_proposal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advancement.major_gift_proposal. Business justification: Engagement activities are the transactional record of cultivation and stewardship interactions. The engagement_activity table has an explicit attribute is_major_gift_related (BOOLEAN) indicating that ']
  primary_engagement_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advancement or alumni relations staff member who recorded or facilitated this engagement activity. Tracks ownership and accountability for relationship management.']
  research_award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.research_award. Business justification: Engagement activities center on research (lab tours, research presentations, symposia). Advancement tracks which research awards are featured in donor cultivation for major gift proposals and stewards']
  activity_date date [note: 'The date on which the engagement activity occurred. Represents the primary business event timestamp for this interaction.']
  activity_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the engagement activity. Tracks whether the activity was completed as planned, cancelled, or is still pending.']
  activity_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the engagement activity took place. Provides granular timing for activities requiring time-of-day tracking.']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the engagement activity. Defines the nature of the interaction between the alumnus and the institution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: event_attendance|volunteer_service|mentorship_session|career_fair_participation|webinar|campus_visit|phone_call|email_response|social_media_interaction|donation_solicitation|survey_response|networking_event|reunion_attendance|board_meeting|committee_participation — promote to reference product]']
  attendee_count varchar [note: 'Number of individuals who participated in the engagement activity. Applicable to group activities such as events, webinars, or meetings. Null for one-on-one interactions.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The communication or interaction channel through which the engagement activity was conducted. Distinguishes the medium of interaction from the activity type.']
  cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Direct cost incurred by the institution to conduct this engagement activity. Includes event costs, travel expenses, materials, or other direct expenditures. Used for ROI analysis and budget tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this engagement activity record was first created in the system. Audit field for data governance and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost amount. Supports multi-currency tracking for institutions with international operations.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Length of the engagement activity in minutes. Applicable to activities with measurable duration such as phone calls, meetings, mentorship sessions, or events.']
  engagement_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score assigned to this activity representing its contribution to the alumnus overall engagement level. Used for engagement scoring models and prospect qualification. Scale and methodology defined by institutional advancement strategy.']
  follow_up_date date [note: 'Target date by which follow-up action should be completed. Nullable if no follow-up is required or scheduled.']
  follow_up_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this engagement activity requires a follow-up action or contact. Used to drive task management and ensure relationship continuity.']
  is_major_gift_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this engagement activity is part of a major gift cultivation, solicitation, or stewardship effort. Used to prioritize high-value donor relationship activities.']
  is_volunteer_activity boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this activity represents volunteer service by the alumnus on behalf of the institution. Used to track volunteer engagement separately from other interaction types.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the engagement activity took place. May include campus building names, city names, or virtual platform identifiers.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified this engagement activity record. Audit field for accountability and change management.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this engagement activity record was last updated. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text notes documenting the content, discussion points, outcomes, and any follow-up actions from the engagement activity. Critical for relationship continuity and institutional memory.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result or outcome of the engagement activity. Captures whether the activity achieved its intended purpose and what next steps may be required.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification assigned to this engagement activity based on strategic importance, donor capacity, or relationship stage. Guides staff time allocation and follow-up urgency.']
  response_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code capturing the alumnus response or reaction to the engagement activity. Used for tracking sentiment and engagement quality across interactions.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this engagement activity record in the source operational system. Enables traceability and reconciliation with source systems.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this engagement activity record originated. Examples include Blackbaud Raisers Edge, Slate CRM, or manual entry systems.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief subject line or title summarizing the purpose or topic of the engagement activity. Provides quick context for the interaction.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created this engagement activity record. Audit field for accountability and data quality tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every meaningful interaction between an alumnus and the institution. Captures activity type (event attendance, volunteer service, mentorship session, career fair participation, webinar, campus visit, phone call, email response, social media interaction), activity date, channel, associated program or campaign, staff member who recorded the interaction, engagement score contribution, and notes. This is the primary engagement history ledger for alumni relations and advancement qualification.'
}

Table advancement.alumni_event {
  alumni_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the alumni event record. Primary key.']
  academic_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to curriculum.academic_program. Business justification: Alumni events are frequently program-specific (Engineering alumni reunion, Business school networking night). Linking events to academic programs enables program-level engagement reporting, targeted i']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advancement.campaign. Business justification: Alumni events frequently serve as fundraising vehicles tied to specific campaigns. The alumni_event product has explicit fundraising attributes: is_fundraising_event (BOOLEAN) and fundraising_goal_amo']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Alumni events are often hosted by academic units/cost centers for budget and expense tracking. Creating alumni_event.cost_center_id to link to finance.cost_center for event cost allocation.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Alumni events occur in specific fiscal periods for budget and expense tracking. Creating alumni_event.fiscal_period_id to link to finance.fiscal_period for period-based reporting.']
  organizing_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.org_unit. Business justification: Alumni events are frequently organized by specific colleges or departments (e.g., Engineering Alumni Night, Business School Reunion). Linking to org_unit enables alumni engagement reporting by academi']
  room_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.room. Business justification: Alumni events held on campus require a specific room assignment for capacity planning, AV setup, and space utilization reporting. alumni_event has venue_name/address for off-campus but no FK for on-ca']
  term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.term. Business justification: Alumni reunion events and homecoming events are scheduled around specific academic terms (commencement, fall/spring terms). Advancement staff align event planning with term calendars for venue booking']
  actual_attendance varchar [note: 'Final count of attendees who participated in the event. Populated after event completion for ROI (Return on Investment) analysis and reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the event record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and event planning timeline analysis.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports international events with local pricing.']
  delivery_mode varchar [note: 'Format of event delivery. In-person requires physical venue; virtual is online-only; hybrid offers both options simultaneously.']
  early_registration_deadline date [note: 'Deadline for early-bird registration pricing. Used to incentivize advance commitments and improve planning accuracy.']
  event_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the event, used in communications and registration systems.']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the event, including agenda highlights, speakers, activities, and value proposition. Used in marketing materials and registration pages.']
  event_end_date date [note: 'The date the event concludes. For single-day events, this equals the start date. For multi-day events (e.g., reunion weekend), this is the final day.']
  event_end_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the event programming concludes, including time zone information.']
  event_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the alumni event as displayed in marketing materials and communications.']
  event_start_date date [note: 'The date the event begins. For single-day events, this is the event date. For multi-day events, this is the first day.']
  event_start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the event programming begins, including time zone information for accurate scheduling across regions.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the event. Controls visibility, registration availability, and reporting inclusion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|planning|registration_open|registration_closed|in_progress|completed|cancelled|postponed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the alumni event by its primary purpose and format. Determines programming, budget allocation, and success metrics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: homecoming|reunion|regional_chapter|career_networking|commencement|donor_recognition|athletic_event|volunteer_service|lecture_series|gala|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fundraising_goal_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Target dollar amount to be raised through the event, if applicable. Null for non-fundraising events.']
  is_fundraising_event boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event has a fundraising component or goal (e.g., gala, donor recognition dinner). True triggers gift tracking and campaign attribution.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the event record. Tracks changes throughout the event lifecycle.']
  organizing_staff_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary event organizer. Used for attendee inquiries and internal coordination.']
  organizing_staff_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary advancement staff member responsible for planning and executing the event.']
  organizing_staff_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary event organizer for urgent inquiries and day-of-event coordination.']
  registration_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of attendees the event can accommodate, based on venue capacity or virtual platform limits. Null indicates unlimited capacity.']
  registration_close_date date [note: 'Date when registration closes. After this date, no new registrations are accepted unless manually overridden.']
  registration_open_date date [note: 'Date when registration becomes available to alumni. Controls visibility in event portals and communications.']
  requires_rsvp boolean [note: 'Indicates whether attendees must formally register or RSVP to attend. True for capacity-limited or catered events; false for open-access events.']
  reunion_class_year varchar [note: 'Graduation year for reunion events (e.g., 1995, 2010). Null for non-reunion events. Used to calculate milestone reunions (5th, 10th, 25th, 50th).']
  target_attendance varchar [note: 'Planned or goal number of attendees for the event. Used for budgeting, venue selection, and success measurement.']
  venue_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the event venue. Business-confidential organizational contact data.']
  venue_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information for the venue (suite, floor, building name). Business-confidential organizational contact data.']
  venue_city varchar [note: 'City where the event venue is located. Used for regional event analysis and alumni engagement mapping.']
  venue_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the venue location (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR). Supports international alumni engagement.']
  venue_name varchar [note: 'Name of the physical location or facility hosting the event (e.g., Alumni Center, Grand Ballroom, Stadium Club). Null for virtual-only events.']
  venue_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the venue. Business-confidential organizational contact data.']
  venue_state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code for the venue location (e.g., CA, NY, ON).']
  virtual_meeting_url varchar [note: 'Web link for accessing the virtual event. Confidential to prevent unauthorized access. Null for in-person-only events.']
  virtual_platform varchar [note: 'Name of the online platform used for virtual or hybrid events (e.g., Zoom, Microsoft Teams, Hopin). Null for in-person-only events.']

  Note: 'Master record for alumni-focused events organized or co-sponsored by the institution. Captures event name, type (homecoming, reunion, regional chapter event, career networking, commencement, donor recognition, athletic event), event date(s), location (venue name, city, state, country), virtual/hybrid flag, registration capacity, registration open/close dates, event cost, associated affinity group or college, and organizing staff. Distinct from the instruction domain\'s class schedule — this covers alumni relations programming.'
}

Table advancement.alumni_event_registration {
  alumni_event_registration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the alumni event registration record. Primary key for this entity.']
  alumni_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the alumni event for which the registration is made. Links to the alumni event master record.']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the alumnus registering for the event. Links to the alumni master record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member who entered the registration on behalf of the alumnus. Null if self-registered by the alumnus.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Alumni event registrations occur in specific fiscal periods for event revenue and attendance tracking. Creating alumni_event_registration.fiscal_period_id to link to finance.fiscal_period for period-b']
  payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment. Business justification: Alumni event registration payments processed through the central billing system should reference billing.payment for reconciliation and audit. Current denormalized payment columns (payment_amount, pay']
  student_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.student_account. Business justification: Alumni event registration fees are commonly billed to a participant\'s institutional student/alumni account in higher-ed. This link enables the billing system to post event charges directly to the acco']
  accessibility_requirements varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any accessibility accommodations needed by the alumnus (e.g., wheelchair access, sign language interpreter, hearing assistance). Ensures ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) compliance.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the registration was cancelled by the alumnus or staff. Null if the registration was not cancelled.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation or categorized reason for registration cancellation (e.g., schedule conflict, illness, duplicate registration). Used for event planning improvement.']
  check_in_method varchar [note: 'Method or channel used to check in the alumnus at the event (e.g., mobile app, QR code scan, manual staff entry).']
  check_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the alumnus checked in at the event venue. Null if the alumnus did not attend. Used for attendance verification and engagement scoring.']
  confirmation_email_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a registration confirmation email was successfully sent to the alumnus.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this registration record was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  dietary_requirements varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any dietary restrictions, allergies, or preferences specified by the alumnus (e.g., vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, nut allergy). Used for catering planning.']
  engagement_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated score representing the level of engagement demonstrated by this registration (e.g., based on early registration, attendance, participation). Used for alumni relationship scoring and predictive analytics.']
  guest_count varchar [note: 'Number of additional guests accompanying the alumnus to the event. Used for capacity planning and catering.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this registration record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  name_badge_printed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a name badge has been printed for the alumnus for this event.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal staff notes or comments about the registration, not visible to the alumnus. Used for operational coordination and special handling instructions.']
  referral_source varchar [note: 'How the alumnus learned about the event (e.g., email invitation, social media, word of mouth, website). Used for marketing effectiveness analysis.']
  registration_campaign_code varchar [note: 'Marketing campaign code or tracking identifier associated with the registration source. Enables ROI (Return on Investment) analysis of promotional efforts.']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date when the alumnus registered for the event. Represents the business event date of registration submission.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique registration confirmation number provided to the alumnus upon successful registration.']
  registration_source varchar [note: 'Channel or interface through which the registration was submitted. Enables analysis of registration channel effectiveness.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the registration. Tracks progression from initial registration through event attendance or cancellation.']
  registration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the registration was submitted, including time zone information. Used for audit and sequence analysis.']
  reminder_email_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a pre-event reminder email was successfully sent to the alumnus.']
  special_requests varchar [note: 'Additional notes or special requests submitted by the alumnus during registration (e.g., seating preferences, program participation).']
  table_assignment varchar [note: 'Assigned table number or seating location for the alumnus at the event. Used for seated events with assigned seating.']
  ticket_type varchar [note: 'Type or category of ticket selected by the alumnus (e.g., general admission, VIP, early bird, complimentary, student rate). Determines pricing and access level.']
  vip_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alumnus is designated as a VIP (Very Important Person) for this event, requiring special handling or recognition.']
  waitlist_added_date date [note: 'Date when the alumnus was added to the event waitlist. Null if never waitlisted.']
  waitlist_position varchar [note: 'Numeric position of the alumnus on the event waitlist if registration status is waitlisted. Null if not on waitlist.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each alumnus\'s registration for an alumni event. Includes registration date, registration status (registered, waitlisted, cancelled, attended, no-show), ticket type, payment amount, payment status, dietary or accessibility requirements, guest count, check-in timestamp, and registration source (online portal, staff entry, mobile app). Enables attendance tracking, event ROI analysis, and engagement scoring.'
}

Table advancement.donor {
  donor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the donor record. Primary key for the donor entity. Serves as the system-of-record identifier in Blackbaud Raisers Edge.']
  alumnus_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advancement.alumnus. Business justification: Donors who are alumni require direct linkage for comprehensive constituent relationship management, lifetime giving analysis, and integrated advancement operations. Enables single-view reporting acros']
  constituent_id varchar [note: 'External constituent identifier from Blackbaud Raisers Edge. Business-facing identifier used for donor lookup and correspondence.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the advancement officer or gift officer assigned to manage the relationship with this donor. Used for portfolio management and accountability.']
  primary_spouse_donor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the donor\'s spouse if the spouse is also a constituent in the database. Used for household giving analysis and joint recognition.']
  address_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of address validation through USPS NCOA or other verification services. Indicates deliverability and accuracy of mailing address.']
  alternate_email varchar [note: 'Secondary email address for donor contact. Used when primary email is unavailable or for specific communication preferences.']
  consecutive_giving_years varchar [note: 'Number of consecutive fiscal years the donor has made a gift. Used to identify loyal donors for recognition societies and retention strategies.']
  constituent_classification varchar [note: 'CASE-compliant constituent classification indicating primary relationship to the institution. Used for segmentation and reporting to CASE and VSE surveys.']
  deceased_date date [note: 'Date of death for deceased donors. Used for memorial gift processing and estate settlement tracking.']
  deceased_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the donor is deceased. When true, suppresses all solicitation communications and triggers estate/planned giving protocols.']
  do_not_contact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates the donor has requested no contact from the institution. When true, suppresses all solicitation, marketing, and non-essential communications. Overrides all other communication preferences.']
  do_not_solicit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates the donor has requested no fundraising solicitations. When true, suppresses appeals and solicitation calls but allows stewardship and informational communications.']
  donor_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the donor record. Active donors have given within the last 24 months. Lapsed donors have not given recently but have historical giving. Deceased and do-not-contact statuses suppress solicitation.']
  donor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the donor entity type. Determines applicable tax treatment, reporting requirements, and stewardship protocols.']
  fiscal_year_giving_total decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total giving from this donor in the current fiscal year. Used for annual fund reporting and donor recognition society qualification.']
  giving_capacity_rating varchar [note: 'Prospect research rating indicating the donor\'s estimated philanthropic capacity. Used for major gift identification and portfolio assignment. Proprietary rating scale from wealth screening vendor.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the donor as it appears on official documents, tax receipts, and gift agreements. For individuals: full legal name. For organizations: registered legal entity name.']
  mobile_phone varchar [note: 'Mobile telephone number for SMS communications and mobile-first outreach. Subject to TCPA consent requirements for text messaging.']
  ncoa_update_date date [note: 'Date when the donor\'s address was last updated through USPS National Change of Address processing. Used to maintain address accuracy and reduce returned mail.']
  portfolio_assignment varchar [note: 'Fundraising portfolio to which the donor is assigned. Indicates which advancement officer or team is responsible for donor cultivation and solicitation.']
  preferred_name varchar [note: 'Name the donor prefers to be addressed by in communications, event materials, and donor recognition. May differ from legal name.']
  primary_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the donor\'s primary mailing address (street number and name). Used for gift acknowledgments, tax receipts, and solicitation mailings.']
  primary_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the donor\'s primary mailing address (apartment, suite, unit, building). Optional field for additional address details.']
  primary_affiliation varchar [note: 'Primary institutional affiliation of the donor (e.g., College of Engineering, School of Business, Athletics). Used for targeted appeals and recognition.']
  primary_city varchar [note: 'City name for the donor\'s primary mailing address. Used for geographic segmentation and regional campaign targeting.']
  primary_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the donor\'s primary mailing address. Used for international donor segmentation and tax compliance.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for donor communications, gift acknowledgments, and electronic correspondence. Subject to CAN-SPAM compliance.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone contact number for the donor. Used for solicitation calls, event invitations, and stewardship outreach.']
  primary_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the donor\'s primary mailing address. Used for address validation, NCOA processing, and geographic analysis.']
  primary_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code for the donor\'s primary mailing address. Two-letter abbreviation for US states or equivalent for international addresses.']
  record_created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this donor record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  record_last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this donor record was last updated. Used for data quality monitoring and change tracking.']
  salutation varchar [note: 'Formal salutation used in correspondence and communications (e.g., Dr., Mr., Mrs., Ms., The Honorable). Respects donor preferences for formal address.']
  stewardship_tier varchar [note: 'Donor stewardship level based on cumulative giving, giving capacity, and engagement. Determines frequency and type of personalized outreach and recognition.']
  wealth_screening_date date [note: 'Date when the donor\'s wealth and capacity rating was last updated through electronic screening or prospect research. Used to ensure rating currency.']
  work_phone varchar [note: 'Business telephone number for corporate donors or individual donors who prefer work contact. Includes extension if applicable.']

  Note: 'Master record for all donors and prospective donors (individuals, corporations, foundations, and organizations). SSOT for donor identity, biographical data, contact information, addresses, communication preferences, giving capacity ratings, relationship classification, and inter-constituent relationships (spouse, employer, parent-child). Sourced from Blackbaud Raisers Edge. Tracks donor type, primary affiliation, CASE-compliant constituent classification, stewardship tier, and portfolio assignment to gift officers. Includes contact details (addresses, phones, emails with type and preference flags) and relationship graph data previously modeled separately.'
}

Table advancement.gift {
  gift_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the gift transaction. Primary key for the gift record.']
  advancement_fund_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the institutional fund or account to which the gift is designated. Links to the fund master record for tracking gift allocation and endowment stewardship.']
  appeal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to advancement.appeal. Business justification: gift has a STRING attribute \'appeal_code\' that should be normalized to a FK relationship. Gifts are often made in response to specific fundraising appeals. The appeal entity already exists in the doma']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.bank_account. Business justification: Gift deposit reconciliation: gifts are deposited into specific institutional bank accounts (lockbox, wire, operating). Treasury and advancement reconcile gift deposits daily by bank account. gift has ']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fundraising campaign associated with this gift. Used for campaign performance tracking and goal attainment reporting.']
  donor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alumnus or external donor who made the gift. Links to the donor master record in the advancement system.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Gifts are recorded in specific fiscal periods for revenue recognition and financial reporting. Creating gift.fiscal_period_id to link to finance.fiscal_period for period-based reporting.']
  gift_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member, volunteer, or alumnus who solicited or facilitated the gift. Used for crediting and performance tracking.']
  gift_officer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advancement staff member responsible for managing the donor relationship and processing this gift. Used for portfolio management and performance tracking.']
  pledge_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pledge record if this gift is a payment toward a multi-year pledge commitment. Links to the pledge master record for tracking fulfillment.']
  research_award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.research_award. Business justification: Gifts often designated for specific research awards or labs. Critical for stewardship reporting, impact metrics, and fund accounting. Advancement must track which gifts support which research for dono']
  revenue_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger_account. Business justification: Gifts post to specific revenue ledger accounts for GL accounting. Creating gift.revenue_ledger_account_id to link to finance.ledger_account for revenue recognition and financial reporting.']
  scholarship_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aid.scholarship. Business justification: Gifts are frequently designated to specific named scholarships; advancement offices must track gift-to-scholarship designation for gift restriction compliance, IPEDS reporting, and donor stewardship. ']
  acknowledgment_date date [note: 'The date the acknowledgment letter or receipt was sent to the donor. Required for IRS substantiation requirements and donor stewardship tracking.']
  acknowledgment_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether a thank-you acknowledgment letter or receipt has been sent to the donor. Critical for donor stewardship and IRS compliance.']
  amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the gift in the transaction currency. For in-kind gifts, this represents the fair market value as appraised or declared.']
  amount_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'The gift amount converted to US Dollars using the exchange rate on the gift date. Used for consolidated reporting and CASE VSE compliance.']
  anonymous_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the donor has requested anonymity for this gift. When true, donor identity is not disclosed in public recognition or reporting.']
  case_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this gift qualifies for inclusion in CASE Voluntary Support of Education (VSE) reporting. Based on CASE counting standards and gift type.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this gift record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the gift amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for international gift processing and multi-currency reporting.']
  deposit_date date [note: 'The date the gift funds were deposited into the institutional bank account. Used for cash flow tracking and financial reconciliation.']
  first_gift_date date [note: 'Date of the donor\'s first recorded gift to the institution. Used to calculate donor tenure and loyalty metrics. [Moved from donor: This attribute represents the date of the first gift transaction, which is a derived value from the gift association. It should be calculated from gift.gift_date rather than stored redundantly on the donor record.]']
  gift_date date [note: 'The date the gift was received or postmarked, representing the official transaction date for accounting and reporting purposes. This is the primary business event timestamp for the gift.']
  gift_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the gift transaction, typically a human-readable sequential or formatted number assigned by the advancement office for tracking and reference purposes.']
  gift_source varchar [note: 'Classification of the donor type: individual, corporation, foundation, organization, or other entity. Used for CASE VSE reporting and donor segmentation.']
  gift_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the gift transaction indicating whether it has been received, is pending processing, or has been cancelled or adjusted.']
  gift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the gift based on its nature: outright gift, pledge payment, matching gift, gift-in-kind, securities transfer, or planned giving instrument.']
  in_kind_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the donated property, goods, or services for in-kind gifts. Required for IRS substantiation and institutional asset management.']
  ipeds_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this gift must be included in IPEDS Finance Survey reporting to the U.S. Department of Education.']
  largest_gift_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of the largest single gift received from this donor. Used for major gift benchmarking and upgrade solicitation strategies. [Moved from donor: This attribute represents the amount of the largest gift transaction, which is a derived value from the gift association. It should be calculated from gift.gift_amount rather than stored redundantly on the donor record.]']
  last_gift_date date [note: 'Date of the donor\'s most recent gift. Used to identify lapsed donors and calculate recency metrics for predictive modeling. [Moved from donor: This attribute represents the date of the most recent gift transaction, which is a derived value from the gift association. It should be calculated from gift.gift_date rather than stored redundantly on the donor record.]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this gift record was most recently updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.']
  lifetime_giving_total decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative total of all gifts received from this donor since the first gift date. Includes cash, pledges paid, and gifts-in-kind at fair market value. Excludes pledge balances. [Moved from donor: This attribute represents the cumulative sum of all gift amounts, which is a derived aggregate from the gift association. It should be calculated from SUM(gift.gift_amount) rather than stored redundantly on the donor record.]']
  matching_gift_company_name varchar [note: 'Name of the corporation or employer that may provide a matching gift for this donation. Used for matching gift claim processing.']
  matching_gift_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this gift is eligible for corporate matching gift programs. Used to trigger matching gift solicitation workflows.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional context, special instructions, or donor communications related to the gift. Used for stewardship and internal coordination.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The instrument or mechanism used to make the gift: cash, check, credit card, wire transfer, ACH, stock transfer, property donation, or other method. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cash|check|credit_card|wire_transfer|ach|stock|property|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number from the payment processor, bank, or credit card transaction. Used for reconciliation and audit trail.']
  purpose varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the donors intended use or purpose for the gift. Captures donor intent for restricted gifts and stewardship reporting.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Unique receipt number issued to the donor for tax purposes. Required for gifts above IRS thresholds and for donor record-keeping.']
  restriction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of gift restrictions per FASB/GASB accounting standards: unrestricted, temporarily restricted, or permanently restricted (endowment).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this gift record originated (e.g., Blackbaud Raisers Edge, manual entry). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this gift record in the source operational system. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and bi-directional synchronization.']
  stock_shares decimal(15,4) [note: 'Number of shares transferred for securities gifts. Used in conjunction with stock price to calculate gift value.']
  stock_symbol varchar [note: 'Ticker symbol for securities gifts. Used for tracking stock transfers and calculating fair market value at the time of transfer.']
  tax_deductible_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The portion of the gift amount that qualifies as tax-deductible under IRS regulations. May differ from gift amount for quid pro quo gifts or benefits received.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every completed gift received by the institution, including outright gifts, matching gifts, gifts-in-kind, and securities transfers. Captures gift date, amount, fund designation, gift type (cash, check, credit card, wire, stock, in-kind), campaign attribution, appeal code, acknowledgment status, and receipting information. SSOT for gift history and the primary source for CASE VSE (Voluntary Support of Education) reporting and IPEDS finance reporting. Sourced from Blackbaud Raiser\'s Edge gift module.'
}

Table advancement.pledge {
  pledge_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pledge record. Primary key.']
  advancement_fund_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the designated fund or endowment to which the pledge is directed.']
  appeal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific fundraising appeal or solicitation effort that resulted in this pledge.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fundraising campaign associated with this pledge.']
  donor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alumnus or constituent who made the pledge commitment.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advancement staff member, volunteer, or alumnus who solicited this pledge.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Pledges are recorded in specific fiscal periods for receivable tracking and financial reporting. Creating pledge.fiscal_period_id to link to finance.fiscal_period for period-based reporting.']
  receivable_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger_account. Business justification: Pledges post to pledge receivable ledger accounts for GL accounting. Creating pledge.receivable_ledger_account_id to link to finance.ledger_account for receivable tracking and financial reporting.']
  research_award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.research_award. Business justification: Pledges designated for research need linkage to specific awards for fulfillment tracking, stewardship, and financial planning. Advancement tracks pledge payments against research funding commitments f']
  scholarship_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aid.scholarship. Business justification: Pledges are designated to specific scholarships; pledge administration requires tracking which scholarship a multi-year pledge funds for installment billing, gift restriction compliance, and scholarsh']
  acknowledgment_date date [note: 'The date on which the pledge acknowledgment letter or receipt was sent to the donor.']
  acknowledgment_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal acknowledgment letter or receipt has been sent to the donor for this pledge.']
  amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total committed amount of the pledge across all installments, representing the donor\'s full commitment.']
  amount_paid decimal(15,2) [note: 'The cumulative amount that has been paid against the pledge to date, calculated from completed installment payments.']
  anonymous_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the donor has requested that this pledge remain anonymous in public recognition and reporting.']
  balance_outstanding decimal(15,2) [note: 'The remaining unpaid balance on the pledge, calculated as pledge_amount minus amount_paid minus write_off_amount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this pledge record was first created in the data platform.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the pledge amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  designation varchar [note: 'The specific purpose, program, or project to which the pledge is designated, such as scholarship fund, building project, or unrestricted support.']
  final_installment_date date [note: 'The due date for the final scheduled installment payment, representing the expected completion date of the pledge.']
  first_installment_date date [note: 'The due date for the first scheduled installment payment.']
  installment_frequency varchar [note: 'The recurring schedule on which pledge installments are due, such as monthly, quarterly, or annually.']
  installments_paid varchar [note: 'The count of installments that have been successfully paid to date.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this pledge record was most recently updated in the data platform.']
  last_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The amount of the most recent installment payment received.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent installment payment was received.']
  matching_gift_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pledge is eligible for corporate matching gift programs.']
  next_installment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The scheduled payment amount for the next upcoming installment.']
  next_installment_date date [note: 'The due date for the next upcoming installment payment, used for reminder workflows and aging analysis.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional context, special conditions, donor preferences, or stewardship considerations related to the pledge.']
  number_of_installments varchar [note: 'The total number of scheduled installment payments for this pledge.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The preferred or default payment instrument the donor will use to fulfill pledge installments, such as check, credit card, or electronic funds transfer (EFT). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: check|credit_card|eft|wire|stock|cash|payroll_deduction — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pledge_date date [note: 'The date on which the donor formally committed to the pledge. This is the principal business event timestamp for the pledge origination.']
  pledge_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier or reference number for the pledge, often used in donor communications and receipts.']
  pledge_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pledge commitment indicating whether it is active, fulfilled, or terminated.']
  pledge_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pledge based on its structure and conditions, such as outright, multi-year installment, or conditional commitment.']
  reminder_date date [note: 'The date on which a payment reminder should be sent to the donor for an upcoming installment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the operational system from which this pledge record was sourced, typically Blackbaud Raiser\'s Edge.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this pledge record in the source operational system.']
  tax_deductible_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The portion of the pledge that qualifies as a tax-deductible charitable contribution under IRS regulations.']
  write_off_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The portion of the pledge that has been written off as uncollectible, reducing the expected receivable.']
  write_off_date date [note: 'The date on which the pledge or a portion thereof was written off as uncollectible.']
  write_off_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or justification for writing off the pledge amount, such as donor financial hardship, death, or inability to contact.']

  Note: 'Record of a donor\'s formal commitment to make a future gift, including multi-year pledge schedules with individual installment detail (due dates, scheduled amounts, payment status per installment), pledge balance, installment frequency, reminder dates, and fulfillment status. Tracks pledge origination date, total amount, amount paid to date, outstanding balance, write-off amounts, and installment-level aging. Supports pledge reminder workflows, automated installment tracking, and aging analysis. Sourced from Blackbaud Raisers Edge pledge module. Distinct from gift (completed transactions) and planned_gift (deferred/estate commitments).'
}

Table advancement.campaign {
  campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fundraising campaign or donor recognition program. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Campaigns often align with academic cost centers for budget allocation and expense tracking. Creating campaign.cost_center_id to link to finance.cost_center for financial reporting and stewardship.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Staff member responsible for coordinating and managing the campaign.']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Campaign fund management: comprehensive campaigns designate one or more finance funds to receive proceeds. Finance and advancement jointly track campaign-to-fund allocation for budget-to-actual report']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Campaigns span fiscal periods and are reported by period for fundraising progress tracking. Creating campaign.fiscal_period_id to link to finance.fiscal_period for period-based reporting.']
  owning_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to hr.org_unit. Business justification: Campaigns are institutional fundraising initiatives requiring org hierarchy linkage for budget ownership, gift allocation reporting, campaign performance dashboards by college/unit, and stewardship ac']
  parent_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent campaign if this is a sub-campaign within a larger comprehensive campaign hierarchy.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Fundraising campaigns require state charitable solicitation registration filings (Unified Registration Statement) in states where the institution solicits. The campaign\'s case_reporting_classification']
  active_membership_count varchar [note: 'Current number of active members in a recognition society. Null for fundraising campaigns.']
  amount_raised decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount raised to date, including gifts, pledges, and planned gifts counted toward the campaign goal.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total budget allocated for campaign operations, marketing, events, and staffing.']
  campaign_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the campaign purpose, objectives, and intended impact.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the fundraising campaign or recognition society.']
  campaign_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the campaign, used in communications and reporting.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the campaign: planning (pre-launch), quiet_phase (leadership gifts), public_phase (broad solicitation), active (ongoing), completed (goal achieved), suspended (temporarily paused), or cancelled (terminated). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planning|quiet_phase|public_phase|active|completed|suspended|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the campaign: comprehensive (institution-wide), capital (facilities/infrastructure), annual (yearly giving), endowment (permanent funds), emergency (crisis response), or recognition_society (donor club/giving circle).']
  case_reporting_classification varchar [note: 'CASE standard classification code for reporting purposes (e.g., comprehensive campaign, capital campaign).']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for campaign inquiries and donor communications.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for campaign office or coordinator.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this campaign record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this campaign (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  donor_count varchar [note: 'Total number of unique donors who have contributed to this campaign.']
  end_date date [note: 'Planned or actual end date of the campaign. Null for ongoing recognition societies.']
  gift_counting_rules varchar [note: 'Description of which gift types are counted toward campaign totals (e.g., outright gifts, pledges, planned gifts, matching gifts).']
  goal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target fundraising amount for the campaign in the specified currency.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this campaign record was last updated.']
  member_benefits_description varchar [note: 'Description of benefits provided to members of a recognition society (e.g., event invitations, publications, naming opportunities).']
  minimum_giving_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum cumulative or annual giving amount required for membership in a recognition society. Null for fundraising campaigns.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or internal documentation about the campaign.']
  phase varchar [note: 'Current operational phase: planning (pre-launch), quiet (leadership solicitation), public (broad outreach), or completion (wrap-up and stewardship).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Institutional priority level assigned to this campaign for resource allocation and strategic focus.']
  public_launch_date date [note: 'Date when the campaign transitioned from quiet phase to public phase and broad solicitation began.']
  quiet_phase_goal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target amount to be raised during the quiet phase before public launch, typically from leadership donors.']
  recognition_tier varchar [note: 'Tier or level within a recognition society hierarchy (e.g., Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum). Applicable to recognition societies.']
  start_date date [note: 'Official start date when the campaign becomes active for gift counting and solicitation.']
  stewardship_plan varchar [note: 'Description of donor stewardship activities and recognition plans for campaign contributors.']
  success_metrics varchar [note: 'Key performance indicators and success criteria for evaluating campaign effectiveness beyond dollar goals.']
  visibility_status varchar [note: 'Controls who can view campaign information: public (all), internal (institution only), or confidential (restricted access).']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website URL for the campaign with information, updates, and online giving portal.']

  Note: 'Master record for fundraising campaigns and donor recognition programs. For campaigns: captures campaign name, type (comprehensive, capital, annual, endowment, emergency), start/end dates, goal amount, amount raised, donor count, phase, sub-campaign hierarchy, and CASE reporting classification. For recognition societies/giving clubs: captures society name, minimum giving threshold, tier, member benefits, and active membership roster. SSOT for campaign definitions and recognition program structures used across gift, pledge, and appeal records.'
}

Table advancement.advancement_fund {
  advancement_fund_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the advancement fund. Primary key for the fund master record.']
  benefiting_student_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studentlife.student_org. Business justification: Donors establish named funds to benefit specific student organizations (e.g., endowed support for student newspaper, Greek chapter scholarship fund). Advancement stewardship reporting requires linking']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Advancement funds map to academic cost centers for stewardship reporting and budget alignment. Creating advancement_fund.cost_center_id to link to finance.cost_center for financial and operational rep']
  designated_academic_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to curriculum.academic_program. Business justification: Advancement funds are designated to support specific academic programs. Fund-to-program reporting, stewardship impact statements, and budget allocation tracking require this link. Role prefix \'designa']
  finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Fund establishment and GL tracking: every advancement fund (endowment, restricted gift fund) maps to a finance fund in the GL. Controllers require this link for fund balance reporting, spending policy']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Advancement funds are reported and valued by fiscal period for financial reporting. Creating advancement_fund.fiscal_period_id to link to finance.fiscal_period for period-based reporting.']
  ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger_account.
