  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the student affairs staff member or administrator who approved the event.']
  event_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Events incur expenses (catering, supplies, facilities) charged to sponsoring department cost centers. Link enables event cost tracking, budget variance analysis, and ensures departments manage event s']
  event_expense_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger_account. Business justification: Event expenses post to specific ledger accounts (programming supplies, contracted services, food service). Link supports expense tracking, budget monitoring, and facilitates proper expense classificat']
  event_finance_fund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_fund. Business justification: Campus event budgets are encumbered and expended against specific finance funds (e.g., student activities fund, auxiliary fund). Direct fund reference enables event budget management, encumbrance trac']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the academic or administrative department co-sponsoring or supporting the event, if applicable.']
  parent_campus_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on campus_event (parent_campus_event_id)']
  room_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the campus facility, room, or outdoor space where the event will be held.']
  student_org_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary student organization, department, or campus life office that is organizing or sponsoring the event.']
  term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.term. Business justification: Campus events are planned and reported within academic terms for programming calendars, co-curricular transcript term attribution, budget cycle alignment, and term-based student engagement analytics. ']
  accessibility_accommodations varchar [note: 'Description of accessibility features and accommodations provided for the event to ensure inclusive participation (e.g., ASL interpretation, wheelchair access, dietary accommodations).']
  actual_attendance varchar [note: 'Actual number of participants who attended the event, recorded after event completion for reporting and assessment.']
  actual_expense_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total actual expenses incurred for the event, recorded after event completion for financial reconciliation and reporting.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the event received formal approval from the appropriate authority to proceed with planning and execution.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event requires formal approval from student affairs administration or a campus event review committee before proceeding.']
  budget_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total budget allocated or approved for the event, covering all anticipated expenses including venue, catering, speakers, materials, and promotion.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or justification for why the event was cancelled or postponed, if applicable.']
  cocurricular_credit_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether attendance at this event qualifies for co-curricular transcript credit or student engagement recognition.']
  cocurricular_credit_hours decimal(4,2) [note: 'Number of co-curricular credit hours awarded to students who attend and complete participation requirements for the event.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact person for the event, used for participant inquiries and event coordination.']
  contact_person_name varchar [note: 'Name of the staff member, advisor, or student leader serving as the primary contact for event inquiries and coordination.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact person for the event, used for urgent inquiries and coordination.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the campus event record was first created in the system.']
  event_category varchar [note: 'Broad categorical grouping of the event for reporting and analytics purposes, distinct from event_type which provides more granular classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: academic|social|cultural|athletic|professional|service|wellness — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the event purpose, agenda, speakers, activities, and learning outcomes for promotional and informational purposes.']
  event_end_datetime timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when the campus event is expected to conclude.']
  event_location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the event venue or location for display and communication purposes.']
  event_name varchar [note: 'Official name or title of the campus event as it appears in promotional materials and event calendars.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the campus event, used for registration, promotion, and tracking purposes.']
  event_start_datetime timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when the campus event is set to begin, representing the principal business event timestamp.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the campus event indicating its progression through planning, approval, execution, and completion stages.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the campus event by its primary purpose or format. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lecture|concert|cultural_celebration|athletic|orientation|leadership_workshop|community_service|social|greek_life|career_fair|academic_symposium|wellness_program|diversity_initiative|student_government|club_meeting|fundraiser|volunteer_opportunity — promote to reference product]']
  expected_attendance varchar [note: 'Estimated number of participants or attendees anticipated for the event, used for planning and resource allocation.']
  hybrid_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event offers both in-person and virtual attendance options simultaneously.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the campus event record was most recently updated or modified.']
  registration_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of registrations or attendees allowed for the event, based on venue capacity or program constraints.']
  registration_close_date date [note: 'Date when registration for the event closes, after which no additional registrations are accepted.']
  registration_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged to students or participants to register for or attend the event, if applicable.']
  registration_open_date date [note: 'Date when registration for the event becomes available to eligible students and participants.']
  registration_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether students or attendees must register in advance to participate in the event.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Description of the intended participant population for the event (e.g., first-year students, graduate students, specific majors, all students, faculty and staff).']
  virtual_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event is conducted entirely online or in a virtual format rather than in-person.']
  virtual_platform varchar [note: 'Name of the online platform or technology used to host the virtual or hybrid event (e.g., Zoom, Microsoft Teams, Canvas Conferences).']

  Note: 'Master record for campus events organized or co-sponsored by student affairs, student organizations, or campus life offices. Captures event name, event type (lecture, concert, cultural celebration, athletic, orientation, leadership workshop, community service, social, Greek life, career fair), sponsoring organization or department, event date and time, event location, expected attendance, registration required flag, registration capacity, event status (planned, approved, active, completed, cancelled), and co-curricular credit eligibility flag. Distinct from admission.recruitment_event and alumni.alumni_event which serve different populations.'
}

Table studentlife.event_attendance {
  event_attendance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the event attendance record. Primary key for the event attendance transaction.']
  academic_term_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the academic term during which the event occurred. Used for co-curricular transcript organization and term-based engagement reporting.']
  campus_event_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the campus event that the student attended. Links to the event master record.']
  corrected_event_attendance_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on event_attendance (corrected_event_attendance_id)']
  primary_event_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the staff member or student worker who processed the check-in, if manual or staff-assisted. Used for operational accountability and training.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the student who attended or registered for the event. Links to the student master record.']
  student_org_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the student organization hosting or sponsoring the event, if applicable. Used for organization engagement tracking and funding accountability.']
  ticket_revenue_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger_account. Business justification: Event ticket revenue (ticket_cost_amount on event_attendance) must be posted to specific revenue ledger accounts for student activities financial reporting. This link enables ticket revenue GL posting']
  accommodation_description varchar [note: 'Description of the special accommodations requested or provided for the student. Confidential information used for service delivery and compliance documentation.']
  attendance_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Calculated duration of the student\'s attendance in minutes, derived from check-in and check-out timestamps. Used to verify minimum attendance requirements for credit eligibility.']
  attendance_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the student checked in to the event. Tracks the technology or process used for attendance verification (card swipe, QR code scan, manual entry by staff, mobile app, kiosk, or NFC tap).']
  attendance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the attendance record, entered by staff or system. Used for operational context and issue resolution.']
  attendance_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier or confirmation number for this attendance record, used for check-in verification and student reference.']
  attendance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the student\'s attendance at the event. Tracks whether the student attended, was a no-show, was waitlisted, cancelled their registration, or is currently checked in.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the student or staff for why the attendance was cancelled. Used for event planning and student engagement analysis.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the student cancelled their event registration or attendance. Used for capacity management and no-show analytics.']
  check_in_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or station where the student checked in (e.g., Main Entrance, Registration Desk A, Mobile Check-In). Used for operational logistics and flow analysis.']
  check_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the student checked in to the event. Represents the principal business event timestamp for attendance verification and capacity tracking.']
  check_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the student checked out from the event. Used to calculate duration of attendance and verify full participation for co-curricular credit eligibility.']
  co_curricular_credit_awarded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether co-curricular credit was awarded to the student for attending this event. Used for co-curricular transcript generation and engagement tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this attendance record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  credit_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric amount of co-curricular credit awarded for this attendance. May represent hours, points, or units depending on institutional policy.']
  credit_type varchar [note: 'Type or category of co-curricular credit awarded for this attendance (e.g., Leadership, Community Service, Cultural Engagement, Professional Development, Wellness). Aligns with institutional co-curricular learning outcomes.']
  credit_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the co-curricular credit amount (hours, points, units, or credits). Provides context for the credit_amount field.']
  feedback_rating varchar [note: 'Numeric rating provided by the student in their event feedback (e.g., 1-5 scale). Used for event quality assessment and continuous improvement.']
  feedback_submitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student submitted post-event feedback or evaluation. Used to track response rates and link to event feedback records.']
  guest_count varchar [note: 'Number of guests the student brought to the event, if guests were permitted. Used for capacity tracking and event planning.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this attendance record was last updated in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  no_show_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student registered for the event but did not attend (no-show). Used for engagement analytics and future event planning.']
  participation_level varchar [note: 'Level or role of the student\'s participation in the event (attendee, active participant, volunteer, presenter, or organizer). Used for differentiated co-curricular credit and leadership tracking.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used by the student to pay for the event ticket (e.g., credit card, student account charge, cash, mobile payment). Used for financial reconciliation and payment analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit-card|debit-card|student-account|cash|mobile-payment|check|voucher — promote to reference product]']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Status of the ticket payment for the event (paid, pending, waived, refunded, or unpaid). Used for financial reconciliation and access control.']
  registration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the student registered for the event. Distinct from check-in; represents the initial commitment or RSVP.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated this attendance record (e.g., Campus Labs Engage, OrgSync, custom event management system). Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  special_accommodation_requested_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student requested special accommodations for attending the event (e.g., accessibility, dietary, language interpretation). Used for compliance and service delivery.']
  ticket_cost_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cost of the event ticket paid by the student, if the event required payment. Zero for free events. Used for revenue tracking and financial reporting.']
  ticket_number varchar [note: 'Unique ticket or confirmation number issued to the student for event entry, if ticketing was used. Used for access control and attendance verification.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of attendance verification by staff or automated process. Indicates whether the attendance has been verified, is pending review, was disputed by the student, or has been corrected.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the attendance record was verified or approved by staff. Used for audit trail and credit processing timelines.']
  waitlist_position varchar [note: 'Position of the student on the event waitlist if attendance status is waitlisted. Used for capacity management and automated notification when space becomes available.']
  waitlist_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the student was added to the event waitlist. Used to determine waitlist priority and manage capacity.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing a student\'s attendance at a campus event, used for co-curricular engagement tracking and event capacity management. Captures check-in timestamp, check-out timestamp, attendance method (swipe, QR scan, manual), co-curricular credit awarded flag, credit type and amount, and attendance status (attended, no-show, waitlisted). Provides the foundational data for co-curricular transcripts and student engagement analytics.'
}

Table studentlife.conduct_case {
  conduct_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the student conduct case record. Primary key for the conduct case entity.']
  academic_standing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to student.academic_standing. Business justification: Conduct officers must cross-reference a student\'s SAP/academic standing when calibrating sanctions and determining eligibility for probation vs. suspension. Mandatory counseling referrals triggered by']
  academic_term_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the academic term during which the alleged incident occurred or the case was initiated.']
  appeal_conduct_case_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on conduct_case (appeal_conduct_case_id)']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the conduct officer or administrator assigned to investigate and adjudicate this case.']
  enrollment_status_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to student.enrollment_status. Business justification: Conduct cases resulting in suspension or expulsion require direct reference to the student\'s enrollment_status record for registrar coordination. Conduct officers and compliance staff use this link in']
  hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to student.hold. Business justification: Conduct cases directly result in institutional holds (registration blocks, diploma blocks, transcript notations). Linking conduct_case to student.hold enables conduct officers and registrars to track ']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the student who is the respondent in this conduct case. Links to the student master record.']
  registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_registration. Business justification: Academic integrity violations (cheating, plagiarism) are a named conduct case type that must reference the specific course registration. Academic integrity adjudication, grade penalty application, and']
  reporting_instructor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to faculty.instructor. Business justification: Academic integrity and student conduct processes require identifying the faculty member who filed a conduct report (e.g., academic dishonesty, classroom disruption). Accreditation and Title IX reporti']
  section_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to curriculum.section. Business justification: Academic integrity conduct cases (plagiarism, cheating) are tied to a specific course section. Academic integrity officers, registrars, and faculty need section-level conduct case tracking for grade a']
  student_term_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.student_term_record. Business justification: Conduct adjudication requires evaluating the student\'s academic standing and enrollment status for the incident term. Suspension and dismissal decisions, FERPA-governed conduct records, and student su']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to instruction.instruction_submission. Business justification: Academic Integrity Conduct Process: when plagiarism or cheating is detected (via turnitin/originality score on a submission), the conduct office opens a case referencing the specific submission as pri']
  violated_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Conduct cases are adjudicated under specific institutional policies (student code of conduct, alcohol policy, academic integrity policy). Conduct officers must reference the exact policy violated. Thi']
  advisor_present_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student was accompanied by an advisor (attorney, parent, or support person) during the hearing process.']
  appeal_decision varchar [note: 'Outcome of the appeal process indicating whether the original finding and sanctions were upheld, modified, or overturned.']
  appeal_decision_date date [note: 'Date when the appeal decision was issued by the appellate authority.']
  appeal_filed_date date [note: 'Date when the student submitted a formal appeal of the conduct case outcome.']
  appeal_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student filed a formal appeal of the conduct finding or sanctions.']
  appeal_grounds varchar [note: 'Basis or rationale provided by the student for appealing the conduct case decision (e.g., procedural error, new evidence, disproportionate sanction).']
  case_closure_date date [note: 'Date when the conduct case was officially closed, either after sanctions were completed, appeal process concluded, or case was dismissed.']
  case_notes varchar [note: 'Internal administrative notes and comments regarding the conduct case, investigation, hearing, or sanction compliance. Confidential staff use only.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable unique case number assigned to this conduct matter for tracking and reference purposes. Typically follows institutional numbering convention (e.g., COND-2024-00123).']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the conduct case indicating where it stands in the adjudication process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reported|under investigation|hearing scheduled|resolved|appealed|closed|dismissed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this conduct case record was first created in the system.']
  finding varchar [note: 'Official determination of responsibility for the alleged conduct violation following the hearing or administrative review.']
  finding_date date [note: 'Date when the official finding of responsibility or non-responsibility was issued.']
  hearing_date date [note: 'Date when the formal conduct hearing was held to adjudicate the alleged violation.']
  hearing_location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the conduct hearing took place (e.g., Student Affairs Office, Zoom meeting).']
  hearing_type varchar [note: 'Type of adjudication process used to resolve the conduct case. Administrative hearings are conducted by a single officer; panel hearings involve multiple decision-makers.']
  incident_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or facility where the alleged conduct violation occurred (e.g., residence hall name, building code, off-campus location description).']
  incident_occurrence_date date [note: 'Date when the alleged conduct violation actually occurred. May differ from the report date if the incident was reported after the fact.']
  incident_report_date date [note: 'Date when the alleged conduct violation was formally reported to the institution. This is the official start of the case record, distinct from when the incident occurred.']
  investigation_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the investigation phase was completed and the case was ready for hearing or administrative resolution.']
  investigation_start_date date [note: 'Date when the formal investigation of the alleged conduct violation began.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this conduct case record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  parent_notification_date date [note: 'Date when parent or guardian notification was sent regarding the conduct case.']
  parent_notification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the students parent or guardian was notified of the conduct case per institutional policy and FERPA exceptions.']
  reporting_party_type varchar [note: 'Category of the individual or entity who reported the alleged conduct violation to the institution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: student|faculty|staff|resident assistant|campus security|external party|anonymous — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sanction_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the student successfully completed all imposed sanctions and fulfilled all conduct case requirements.']
  sanction_completion_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the student must complete all imposed sanctions (e.g., finish community service hours, complete educational program).']
  sanction_completion_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the students progress in fulfilling the imposed sanctions.']
  sanction_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of all sanctions imposed, including specific requirements, conditions, and expectations for completion.']
  sanction_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the imposed sanctions take effect and the student must begin compliance.']
  sanction_primary varchar [note: 'Primary disciplinary sanction imposed on the student if found responsible for the conduct violation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: warning|probation|suspension|expulsion|educational program|community service|restitution|housing removal|no contact order — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sanction_secondary varchar [note: 'Additional sanctions imposed beyond the primary sanction. May include multiple requirements such as educational programs, community service hours, or counseling.']
  student_attendance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the respondent student attended the scheduled conduct hearing.']
  transcript_notation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the conduct violation and sanctions result in a notation on the students academic transcript per institutional policy.']
  transcript_notation_text varchar [note: 'Specific text that appears on the students academic transcript if a notation is required (e.g., Disciplinary Suspension, Conduct Probation).']
  violation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the alleged conduct violation, including circumstances, witnesses, and evidence summary. Confidential case information.']
  violation_type_primary varchar [note: 'Primary category of the alleged student code of conduct violation. Represents the most serious or predominant charge in cases with multiple allegations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: academic dishonesty|alcohol|drug|harassment|physical altercation|property damage|noise disruption|theft|unauthorized access — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  violation_type_secondary varchar [note: 'Additional violation category if the case involves multiple alleged infractions. Free-text or comma-separated list of secondary charges.']

  Note: 'Master record for a student conduct case initiated under the institution\'s student code of conduct. Captures incident report date, case number, alleged violation type(s) (academic dishonesty, alcohol/drug, harassment, physical altercation, property damage, noise/disruption), case status (reported, under investigation, hearing scheduled, resolved, appealed, closed), hearing type (administrative, panel), hearing date, finding (responsible, not responsible, deferred), sanction(s) imposed, sanction completion deadline, appeal filed flag, and case closure date. Complements compliance.title_ix_case which handles Title IX-specific matters; this record covers the broader student conduct adjudication process.'
}

Table studentlife.conduct_sanction {
  conduct_sanction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the conduct sanction record. Primary key for the conduct sanction entity.']
  account_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.account_hold. Business justification: Unpaid restitution or conduct fines trigger account holds preventing registration and transcript release. Links sanction to billing hold for financial enforcement of conduct penalties and hold release']
  conduct_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent conduct case that resulted in this sanction. A single conduct case may result in multiple sanctions.']
  conduct_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Conduct office restitution revenue and sanction-related costs are tracked by cost center for Dean of Students departmental budget reporting. Higher-ed institutions allocate conduct program costs and r']
  course_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to curriculum.course. Business justification: Academic integrity sanctions (grade penalties, course failure) are tied to the specific course where the violation occurred. Academic integrity offices and registrars track course-level sanctions for ']
  hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to student.hold. Business justification: conduct_sanction has hold_placed_flag and hold_type (denormalized) but no FK to student.hold. When a sanction places a hold, the authoritative hold record must be referenced for registration, diploma,']
  housing_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studentlife.housing_assignment. Business justification: Specific sanctions include housing removal, reassignment, or restrictions (banned from specific halls). Conduct system must link sanction to affected housing assignment for enforcement. Housing staff ']
  primary_conduct_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member or conduct officer who assigned this sanction to the student.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the student who is subject to this sanction. Links to the student master record.']
  restitution_ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: Conduct restitution and fines generate AR invoices in higher-ed student conduct operations. conduct_sanction has restitution_amount and restitution_paid_amount fields; linking to the AR invoice enable']
  restitution_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger_account. Business justification: Conduct fines and restitution revenue must be posted to specific NACUBO-classified ledger accounts (e.g., fines and forfeitures revenue). This link enables proper GL posting of conduct-related revenue']
  student_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.student_account. Business justification: Restitution amounts and conduct-related fines are billed to student accounts. Links sanction financial penalties to billing system for charge posting, payment tracking, and hold enforcement until rest']
  superseded_conduct_sanction_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on conduct_sanction (superseded_conduct_sanction_id)']
  tertiary_conduct_waived_by_staff_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staff member who authorized the waiver of this sanction. Null if sanction was not waived.']
  training_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.training_program. Business justification: Mandatory training is one of the most common conduct sanctions in higher ed (alcohol education, sexual misconduct prevention, hazing awareness). conduct_sanction.sanction_type covers this but no FK ex']
  appeal_deadline_date date [note: 'Last date by which the student may submit an appeal of this sanction. Null if sanction is not appealable.']
  appeal_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student has the right to appeal this sanction under the institution\'s conduct policies.']
  assigned_date date [note: 'Date when the sanction was officially assigned to the student following the conduct case resolution.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the student fulfilled all requirements of the sanction, as verified by the conduct office or designated staff member.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this sanction record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for restitution amounts. Typically USD for U.S. institutions.']
  due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the student must complete the sanction requirements. Null for sanctions without a completion requirement such as warnings.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the sanction expires or is no longer in effect. Relevant for time-bound sanctions such as probation periods or no-contact orders.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the sanction becomes active and enforceable. May differ from assigned date if the sanction is deferred or scheduled to begin at a future date.']
  external_agency_reported_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sanction or the underlying conduct case was reported to an external agency such as law enforcement, accreditation bodies, or regulatory authorities.']
  hold_placed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a registration or administrative hold was placed on the student\'s account as part of this sanction enforcement.']
  hours_completed decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of hours the student has completed toward the sanction requirement. Updated as progress is documented.']
  hours_required decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of hours required for completion, applicable to community service, educational programs, or counseling sessions. Null for sanctions without hour requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this sanction record was last updated, reflecting any changes to status, completion progress, or other attributes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments regarding the sanction, its administration, or special circumstances. Confidential internal documentation.']
  parent_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether institutional policy requires notification to the student\'s parent or guardian for this sanction, typically for serious violations or for students under age 21 in alcohol/drug cases.']
  parent_notification_sent_date date [note: 'Date when notification was sent to the student\'s parent or guardian. Null if notification was not required or not yet sent.']
  restitution_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary amount the student is required to pay as restitution for damages, losses, or costs incurred. Null for non-financial sanctions.']
  restitution_paid_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Amount of restitution the student has paid to date. Updated as payments are received and processed.']
  sanction_category varchar [note: 'Broader classification grouping of the sanction type for reporting and analytics purposes.']
  sanction_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the specific sanction requirements, conditions, and expectations imposed on the student.']
  sanction_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the sanction, typically formatted as a human-readable reference number used in correspondence and documentation.']
  sanction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sanction indicating whether the student has fulfilled the requirements.']
  sanction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sanction imposed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: disciplinary_warning|disciplinary_probation|educational_program|community_service|restitution|suspension|expulsion|housing_removal|no_contact_order|transcript_notation|loss_of_privileges|deferred_suspension — promote to reference product]']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the sanction severity, used for reporting, trend analysis, and determining escalation paths for repeat offenders.']
  transcript_notation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sanction results in a notation on the student\'s academic transcript, typically for serious violations such as suspension or expulsion.']
  transcript_notation_text varchar [note: 'Exact text that appears on the student\'s transcript if transcript notation is required. Null if no notation applies.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sanction completion was verified by the designated staff member.']
  waiver_date date [note: 'Date when the sanction waiver was approved. Null if sanction was not waived.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the sanction was waived or modified, if applicable. Null if sanction was not waived.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a specific sanction imposed on a student as an outcome of a conduct case. Captures sanction type (disciplinary warning, disciplinary probation, educational program, community service hours, restitution, suspension, expulsion, housing removal, no-contact order), sanction description, assigned date, due date, completion date, completion status (pending, completed, not completed, waived), and verifying staff member. A single conduct case may result in multiple sanctions, each tracked independently with its own completion lifecycle.'
}

Table studentlife.service_learning_placement {
  service_learning_placement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service learning placement record. Primary key for this entity.']
  academic_term_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the academic term during which this service learning placement occurs.']
  course_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the academic course to which this service learning placement is linked, if the placement is course-embedded. Null if the placement is purely co-curricular.']
  degree_progress_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to student.degree_progress. Business justification: Service learning placements awarding academic credit must be tracked against degree_progress to ensure hours count toward graduation requirements. Academic advisors and degree audit systems use this l']
  degree_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to curriculum.degree_requirement. Business justification: Degree audit systems must verify that a completed service-learning placement satisfies a specific degree requirement (e.g., a mandatory community engagement or experiential learning requirement). Regi']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member or administrator who approved this service learning placement.']
  instructor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the faculty member or academic staff who supervises and evaluates the students service learning placement from the institutional side.']
  placement_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Service learning programs operate within academic department cost centers. Placements drive program costs (transportation, insurance, site coordination) and may be grant-funded. Link enables program c']
  prior_service_learning_placement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on service_learning_placement (prior_service_learning_placement_id)']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the student assigned to this service learning placement. Links to the student master record.']
  registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_registration. Business justification: Service-learning placements carrying academic credit must link to the course registration record to post credit hours, grades, and SAP-applicable hours. Academic records offices require this link to p']
  research_award_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.research_award. Business justification: Community-engaged research awards (e.g., NSF, NIH community grants) directly fund student service-learning placements. Grants administrators must link placements to the funding award for budget tracki']
  research_irb_protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to research.irb_protocol. Business justification: Service-learning placements involving human subjects research require IRB protocol approval before student participation. Universities must verify IRB compliance for student research activities to mee']
  section_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to curriculum.section. Business justification: Service-learning placements are supervised at the section level — the specific instructor and term section determines grading, supervision, and hour verification. Faculty and service-learning coordina']
  slo_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to curriculum.slo. Business justification: Accreditation bodies (HLC, SACSCOC) require evidence that service-learning experiences are directly mapped to student learning outcomes. The plain-text learning_objectives field is a denormalized SLO ']
  student_org_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to studentlife.student_org. Business justification: Service learning placements can be organized, coordinated, or sponsored by student organizations (e.g., a community service organization coordinates volunteer placements, an environmental club organiz']
  tuition_charge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.tuition_charge. Business justification: Service learning placements may have associated course fees or lab charges. Links placement to specific tuition charge for financial tracking of course-related fees and refund calculation if placement']
  academic_credit_hours decimal(4,2) [note: 'Number of academic credit hours associated with this service learning placement if it is embedded within a for-credit course. Null for purely co-curricular placements.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the student completed or terminated service activities at the placement site, which may differ from the planned end date.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the service learning placement was officially approved by the institution or program coordinator.']
  background_check_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the student completed the required background check or clearance for this service learning placement.']
  background_check_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service learning site requires the student to complete a background check or clearance before beginning service activities.']
  co_curricular_credit_awarded decimal(4,2) [note: 'Amount of co-curricular credit or recognition units awarded to the student upon successful completion of this service learning placement, if applicable to the institutions co-curricular transcript or recognition program.']
  completed_service_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total number of service hours the student has completed at this placement site as of the last update, tracked toward the required hours commitment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when this service learning placement record was first created in the system.']
  hours_verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify and document the students completed service hours, such as paper timesheet, supervisor confirmation, student self-report, or electronic tracking system.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when this service learning placement record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  liability_waiver_signed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student has signed the required liability waiver or release form for participation in this service learning placement.']
  placement_approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the service learning placement by the institution or program coordinator, indicating whether the placement has been approved, is pending review, denied, or conditionally approved.']
  placement_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the student is scheduled to complete service activities at the placement site. Represents the planned conclusion of the service learning commitment.']
  placement_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the service learning placement, including special arrangements, accommodations, or observations by staff or faculty.']
  placement_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier or reference number for this service learning placement, used for tracking and communication purposes.']
  placement_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the student begins service activities at the placement site. Represents the effective start of the service learning commitment.']
  placement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service learning placement indicating whether it is pending approval, actively in progress, completed, cancelled by the institution, or withdrawn by the student.']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service learning placement indicating whether it is embedded within a course, part of co-curricular programming, a hybrid model, or an independent study arrangement.']
  reflection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student is required to submit written or oral reflections on their service learning experience as part of the placement requirements.']
  reflection_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the student submitted their service learning reflection materials for review.']
  reflection_submission_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the students reflection submission requirement, indicating whether reflection is not required, pending submission, submitted and under review, approved, or requires revision.']
  required_service_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total number of service hours the student is required to complete at this placement site to fulfill course or co-curricular requirements.']
  site_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the street address for the service learning site location.']
  site_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the street address for the service learning site location, typically used for suite or unit numbers.']
  site_city varchar [note: 'City where the service learning site is located.']
  site_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact person at the service learning site for coordination and communication purposes.']
  site_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person at the service learning site, typically the on-site supervisor or coordinator.']
  site_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact person at the service learning site.']
  site_contact_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the primary contact person at the service learning site.']
  site_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the service learning site location.']
  site_name varchar [note: 'Name of the community organization, agency, or institution where the student is performing service learning activities.']
  site_organization_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service learning site organization by sector type, such as nonprofit, government agency, K-12 educational institution, healthcare facility, faith-based organization, corporate entity, or other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: nonprofit|government|k12_education|healthcare|faith_based|corporate|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  site_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the service learning site location.']
  site_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the service learning site is located.']
  transportation_provided_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the institution provides transportation assistance to the student for travel to and from the service learning site.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a student\'s placement in a community service or service-learning site as part of a co-curricular or course-embedded service requirement. Captures placement site name, site organization type (nonprofit, government, K-12, healthcare), placement start and end dates, required service hours, completed service hours, supervisor name and contact, learning objectives, reflection submission status, and co-curricular credit awarded. Tracks the operational fulfillment of service-learning commitments distinct from academic course enrollment.'
}

Table studentlife.health_appointment {
  health_appointment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for health_appointment']
  appointment_charge_ledger_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger_account. Business justification: Health appointment charges (copays, service fees) are posted to specific revenue ledger accounts in university health centers. This link supports charge posting, revenue recognition by service type, a']
  building_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the campus health facility or clinic location where the appointment is scheduled.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the healthcare provider (physician, nurse, counselor, therapist) assigned to conduct the appointment.']
  followup_health_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on health_appointment (followup_health_appointment_id)']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the student who scheduled or attended the health appointment.']
  room_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to facility.room. Business justification: Student health centers track exam room utilization, scheduling efficiency, and infection control by specific room. Linking health_appointment to facility.room replaces the denormalized room_number tex']
  term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to enrollment.term. Business justification: Health center utilization is tracked by academic term for ACHA benchmarking reports, staffing and capacity planning, and student wellness program assessment. Term-based appointment volume reporting is']
  actual_end_time timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the health appointment concluded.']
  actual_start_time timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the health appointment began, as recorded during check-in or service delivery.']
  appointment_notes varchar [note: 'Additional clinical or administrative notes recorded during or after the health appointment.']
  appointment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique appointment reference number used for scheduling and tracking purposes.']
  appointment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the health appointment indicating its progression through the care delivery workflow.']
  appointment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the health appointment based on the nature of service provided.']
  billing_code varchar [note: 'The billing or procedure code associated with the health appointment for financial and insurance processing.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'The reason provided for cancelling the health appointment, if applicable.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the health appointment was cancelled.']
  cancelled_by varchar [note: 'Indicates the party who initiated the cancellation of the health appointment.']
  charge_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total charge amount in US dollars for the health appointment services rendered.']
  check_in_time timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the student checked in for the health appointment at the facility reception.']
  chief_complaint varchar [note: 'The primary reason or symptom reported by the student for seeking the health appointment.']
  confirmation_method varchar [note: 'The communication channel used to confirm the health appointment with the student.']
  consent_date date [note: 'The date on which informed consent was obtained from the student.']
  consent_obtained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether informed consent for treatment or services was obtained from the student.']
  copay_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The copayment amount in US dollars required from the student for the health appointment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the health appointment record was first created in the system.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The scheduled or actual duration of the health appointment measured in minutes.']
  follow_up_date date [note: 'The recommended or scheduled date for the follow-up appointment, if applicable.']
  follow_up_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up appointment or additional care is required based on the outcome of this appointment.']
  insurance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the students health insurance coverage has been verified prior to the appointment.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'The preferred language for communication and service delivery during the health appointment.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the health appointment record was last modified or updated.']
  no_show_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the student failed to attend the scheduled health appointment without prior cancellation.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'The current status of payment processing for the health appointment charges.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'The urgency classification of the health appointment indicating the required response time.']
  referral_source varchar [note: 'The origin or channel through which the student was referred for the health appointment.']
  reminder_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an appointment reminder notification was sent to the student.']
  reminder_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the appointment reminder notification was sent to the student.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the health appointment is scheduled to occur.']
  scheduled_end_time timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the health appointment is scheduled to end.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the health appointment is scheduled to begin.']
  telehealth_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health appointment was conducted via telehealth or virtual consultation rather than in-person.']
  telehealth_platform varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the telehealth platform or technology used for virtual appointments.']
  visit_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the purpose or clinical reason for the health appointment.']
  wait_time_minutes varchar [note: 'The duration in minutes between check-in and the actual start of the appointment, used for service quality monitoring.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for health_appointment. Referenced by appointment_id.'
}
